observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management require additional payment for the uncollected penalties during CY 2019. Thereafter, strictly implement with the penalty provisions of the current Tax Ordinance, requiring assessment and collection of penalties for delayed payments of business taxes as additional income or funds for projects to be implemented as well as ensure earlier collection of the said funds.
observations.managementAction: Treasury Office already ensures strict collections of penalty considering compromises as per tax ordinance.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that Management strategically schedule GAD projects and vigilantly monitor its implementation so that its purpose will be actually realized and enjoyed by majority of the community during the year.
observations.managementAction: GAD Focal Person is now tactically monitors implementation of planned GAD projects which manifest an above 80% utilization since CY2022.

The management commits to comply with the recommendations to properly reflect the correct balance of the RPT and SET Receivables accounts. The management is planning to have an RPT System to fast track the reconciliation.
observations.reason: The management was not able to reconcile the records.

observations.managementAction: The management commits to comply with the recommendations to properly reflect and support the balance of Land Account presented on the FS.
observations.reason: The management has applied for Administrative Reconstitution of the Titles and coordinated with the concerned government agency. As per LRA Isabela, the petition is for endorsement to the LRA Central Office.

observations.managementAction: The management commits to submit the required list of delinquent taxpayers once it is finalized and will comply with the other recommendations mentioned.
observations.reason: No submission made by the management.
Restated in Part II, page 78.

observations.managementAction: The management did not include Communication Allowance to its rank and file employees for Budget Year 2024.
observations.reason: No guideline on the allocations of mobile communications and data services for its employees was crafted by the Management.

observations.managementAction: The management is consistently communicating the matter to the contractor but the latter is requesting for reconsideration.
The management commits to submit the required list of delinquent taxpayers once it is finalized and will comply with the other recommendations mentioned.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to record the RPT and SET transactions as prescribed in the NGAS Manual for LGUs. Likewise, agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to provide an updated Certified List of Real Property Tax Delinquencies to the Accountant annually before the close of accounting books and at the same time perform periodic reconciliation of the RPT and SET Receivables with the Accounting.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant began reconciling the balances in the affected accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still reconciling the balances in the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of all PPEs of the Municipality to comply with the One-time Cleansing of PPE balances and the Local Chief Executive file the request for authority to derecognize non-existing/ missing PPEs to the COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) and/ or the Supervising Auditor (SA) as of provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, if any; the Accounting Office to consistently prepare and update the PPE Ledger Cards and to establish internal control to ensure accuracy of depreciation; and the G0eneral Service Officer-Designate to prepare and Property Cards for each type of asset in accordance with the prescribed format pursuant to NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant began reconciling the balances in the affected accounts.
observations.reason: The reason for the non- implementation is due to the lack of man power.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant strictly observe the deadline of submission of accounts and reports to the Commission on Audit and agreed to submit lacking disbursement vouchers the soonest possible.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant began reconciling the balances in the affected accounts.
observations.reason: The reason for the non- implementation is due to the lack of man power and the bulk of documents that had to be facilitated.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to adopt measures such as availing of the remedies by administrative action through levy on real property or by judicial action to collect delinquent taxes as provided in RA 7160. Further, agreed to strengthen and intensify its tax collection system and tax campaign to minimize tax delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accounting will ensure recording of Real Property Tax (RPT)/ Special Education Tax (SET) Receivables and Deferred RPT/SET Receivables in accordance with the prescribed NGAS Manual for LGUs.
observations.reason: The Municipality is still in the process of reviewing the delinquencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that the requisite information is contained in purchase order and submit the same and all related supporting documents within five (5) days after its perfection for evaluation and review. A similar observation was reported under Finding No. 12 in page 51 of this AAR.
observations.managementAction: BAC secretariat shall be directed to furnish the COA with the copy of the Purchase Order.
observations.reason: The Management still did not comply with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended and Management agreed to:
1.7.1 Ensure that unreleased checks as of year-end are reported and submitted to the Accounting Unit as basis for the reversion of the original accounting entries made;
1.7.2 Take up the necessary adjusting entries to reverse the accounting entries taking up the transfer of unspent LDRRMF to Trust Fund in the General Fund books and the recognition of Trust Liabilities-DRRMF in the Trust Fund books to fairly present the balances of the affected accounts in the financial statements; and
1.7.3 Take up the transfer of unspent LDRRMF as Trust Liabilities in the Trust Fund books only when actually received as evidenced by an official receipt.

2.5 We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
2.5.1 Record purchases of various inventories in the appropriate Inventory Accounts in accordance with the perpetual inventory method as prescribed in Section 114 of Volume 1 of NGAS for LGU; and
2.5.2 Take up the issuances in the appropriate expense account only when completely supported with duly accomplished Requisition Issuance Slip, RSMI or Distribution List.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to adequately provide a concise narrative pertaining to the amount and nature of restricted cash balances under Note 4—Cash and Cash Equivalents—to ensure that relevant information is available to the intended users of the financial statements.

During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant admitted that he overlooked to including the disclosure about the restricted cash balances. Nevertheless, the Management agreed to comply with the foregoing audit recommendation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the following:
Municipal Assessor to reconcile records with the Acting Municipal Treasurer by providing a copy of the complete and updated list of assessed values of taxable and exempt Real Property Units to the latter;
Acting Municipal Treasurer to use the list provided by the Municipal Assessor in updating the Real Property Account Register (RPTAR)/Taxpayer’s Index Card (TIC), which will be used as the basis in the preparation of the revised list of RPT receivables, and furnish the revised list to the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year; and
Municipal Accountant to prepare the following correcting entries:
GF Books
SEF Books
The Management agreed to comply with the foregoing audit recommendations prospectively.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to conduct periodic reconciliation of the RAAO and accounting records to ensure faithful representation of financial statements.
During the exit conference, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to conduct periodic reconciliations of the RAAO and accounting records.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Restate the CY 2023 Statement of Financial Performance by deducting the amount of ₱3,010,579.30 in the Subsidy to Other Funds account and adding ₱2,802,799.77 in the Transfers of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds account to present the financial statement fairly; and
Henceforth, apply the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units in recording financial transactions of the Municipality in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009.
The Municipal Accountant agreed to restate the CY 2023 Financial Statements to reflect the correct account to be used for the recording of the transfer of unspent current year DRRM funds to the Trust Funds, and he signified the application of the revised chart of accounts prospectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Continue to trace the unreconciled balances; and
Conduct regular reconciliation of the accounts to ensure that the balances of reciprocal accounts are the same at all times.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately transfer the cash items, totaling ₱3,561,549.34, to its intended books.
Management signified their agreement to comply with the audit recommendations presented.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to prepare a Physical Inventory Plan containing the specific assignments/duties of the Committee members, the cut-off date and a schedule specifying the dates and locations of the inventory taking activities from start up to the targeted completion of the physical inventory.
Thereafter, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the Inventory Committee, GSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to undertake the procedural guidelines enumerated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances to come up with more reliable account balances to be presented in the financial statements.
Lastly, we recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the GSO - Designate and Municipal Accountant to prepare PC and PPELC, respectively, for each class of PPE as required in NGAS for LGUs by at least starting with those recently acquired through Acceptance and Inspection Reports, PAR, Disbursement Vouchers, supporting documents and other reports.
During the exit conference, Management acknowledged difficulties in implementing the one-time cleansing of PPE because the GSO-Designate and other key personnel being overburdened with their additional duties. However, they have addressed this by creating a Plantilla Position for supply officer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct both the Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to:
Maintain updated inventory records based on the receipt, acceptance, and issuance of inventory items as prescribed by the Manual;
Strictly follow the procedures set forth in Manual; and
Conduct semestral (or oftener depending on the discretion of management) physical count of inventory items to be reported in the RPCI which should be submitted to the Audit Team’s office.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider allocating a room or space for stockroom of inventory items purchased.
Since a supply officer has already been appointed, Management signified compliance with the foregoing audit recommendations. Additionally, Management is already looking for an available space within the vicinity of the Municipal Hall for their stockroom.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MSWDO to review and re-evaluate the existing policies and procedures on the AICS Program and adopt a written policy approved by the Sangguniang Bayan on the determination as to its appropriateness considering the nature, eligibility of beneficiaries, amount of financial assistance, and other measures that would facilitate the effective and efficient implementation of the AICS Program of the municipality.
During the exit conference, Management, through its MSWDO, mentioned that they have already drafted its internal guidelines for their AICS program. They committed in finalizing the said guidelines in CY 2024.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct:
The BAC to remind prospective bidders during the pre-bid conference of the completeness of information in the required Class "A" documents, particularly the completeness of declared ongoing and awarded but not yet started government and private contracts and the accuracy of declared NFCC. Otherwise, disqualify non-compliant bidders during bid evaluation or post qualification based on the grounds set under the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184; and
BAC and TWG to judiciously conduct post-qualification by verifying, validating, and ascertaining all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid; and ensuring that the Post-Qualification Report contains in-depth results on the verification of all requirements by extending the usual post-qualification period.
The Management, particularly the BAC-TWG, acknowledged their inadequate conduct of post-qualification for these projects. However, they pledged to conduct thorough and comprehensive post-qualification processes onwards.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to work closely with the Provincial Government of La Union (PGLU) to streamline the process of aggregating data into the eRPTS system by developing protocols for the timely transfer of assessment rolls and other relevant data from PGLU to MAssO and MTO to ensure consistency and accuracy in RPT reporting.
The Management signified compliance with the foregoing audit recommendation. The Acting Municipal Treasurer commits that he will prepare a letter addressed to the Provincial Government to address the issue.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to make a representation with the Provincial Treasurer’s Office to seek assistance in the enforcement of collection of delinquent taxes, especially those long over-due.  Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor ask for the assistance of the Barangay Officials in informing the heirs of the deceased delinquent taxpayers or occupants of the properties to pay their delinquent taxes.
The Acting Municipal Treasurer explained that he had already communicated orally the dilemma to the Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO). As of to date, the Acting Municipal Treasurer is still waiting for the actions of the PTO.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the completion of the Implementing Rules and Regulations in the imposition of Environmental Fees to augment the income of the Municipality.
During the exit conference, there were no clarificatory questions raised. The Management signified compliance with the audit recommendation presented.
We commended the Management for its efforts in adhering with the pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 10121 by ensuring a high utilization rate of the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary Adjusting Journal Entry (AJE) to correct the misstated accounts in conformity to COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015 to come up with a more accurate and reliable financial information pursuant to Section 27 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 1.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to immediately evaluate and determine the transaction/s which caused the variance and conduct reconciliation thereon pursuant to COA Circular 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 and Section 74 of P.D. No 1445 to fairly present the cash balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the subsidiary ledgers to determine, at any given time, the details and status of the accounts and to facilitate verification of the Financial Statements. Also, submit the required records as provided under Sections 2.0 and 4.1 of COA Circular 2010-001 dated March 2, 2010.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Management direct the Inventory Committee to conduct the annual physical inventory and submit the complete and accurate RPCPPE to the Audit Team pursuant to Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. Thereafter, require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records. Also, we recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal GSO to promptly and meticulously post the acquisition of PPEs to the respective ledger cards and property cards in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal GSO-Designate and the Municipal Assessor to secure available documents to trace and determine the details and breakdown of the unaccounted Land balance of P1.114 million. Moreover, we recommended that Management conduct the necessary One-Time Cleansing of all the Property, Plant and Equipment in accordance with COA Circular 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We recommended that Management monitor and fast-track the registration and transfer of the title of land in the name of LGU Luna, Isabela to have conclusive evidence of ownership and to fully protect the interest of the Municipality in adherence to Section 39(2) of PD No. 1445 and Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992. Moreover, we recommended that Management furnish the Audit Team with electronic copies of the Owner's Duplicate Copy of the TCTs for its review and evaluation.

We recommended that Management ensure the timely and full implementation of the projects, cause the immediate liquidation of the funds transferred and return the unexpended balances to the source agencies upon completion of the projects to fully settle the obligations. As to the unexpended balance of PDAF amounting to P22,624.00, we recommended that Management immediately remit the said amount to the Bureau of Treasury (BTr) in compliance with the pronouncements of the Supreme Court in GR Nos. 208566, 208493 and 209251.

We recommended that the Management immediately submit the required authority from the Sangguniang Bayan for the expenditures totaling ₱5.768 million charged against the "Construction and Rehabilitation of Roads" appropriated under the 20% Development Fund. Moreover, we recommended that Management require the Local Development Council (LDC) to enumerate in the revised Annual Investment Plan (AIP) the specific Programs, Projects and Activities (PPAs) to set a clear disbursement limit and to avoid delay in the implementation thereof.

We recommended that the Management ensure the optimum utilization of the 20% Development Fund by implementing all of the development projects during the year. Moreover, we recommended that Management monitor the implementation of the projects to attain the desired benefits as envisioned in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2020-1 of DBM-DOF-DILG.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (MDRRMC) to prepare the LDRRMFIP by adopting the prescribed format in the Annex A of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to monitor and ensure the actual cash transfer at the end of the year of the unexpended balance of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE to a Special Trust Fund in compliance with the pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 10121. Moreover, we recommended that Management ensure that the LDDRM Fund is properly monitored and that the accounting and reporting guidelines on the unspent balance of the LDRRMF as prescribed in Sections 5.1.10 and 5.1.13 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 are fully complied with.

We recommended that Management:
Require the concerned contractors to immediately repair, rectify or correct the deficiencies noted in the inspection and conform with the approved program of works otherwise, require them to refund the equivalent amount of the deficiencies to prevent issuance of Notice of Disallowance (ND) by the COA Audit Team;
Create an Inspectorate Team that is tasked among others to conduct preliminary inspection once the project reaches an accomplishment of ninety-five (95%) of the total contract amount and submit punch-list of the remaining works, work deficiencies for necessary corrections, and the specific duration/time to fully complete the project considering the approved remaining contract time; and
Direct the Municipal Engineer and the Inspectorate Team to henceforth conduct regular and strict monitoring and supervision of all LGU implemented infrastructure projects to ensure that these are accomplished in accordance with the approved program of works and completed within their specified contract time to fully achieve the intended benefits of these projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer observe the instructions provided in the abovementioned regulation with regard to the orderly and systematic issuance of accountable forms.
We recommended that Management secure adequate bonding for the Liquidating Officer and Collecting Officer with the Bureau of Treasury to ensure that the LGU is duly and adequately indemnified in the event of embezzlement or loss of funds under the custody of the said accountable officers.
We recommended that the LGU strengthen its internal control on the safekeeping of funds by purchasing and installing a safe/vault where the Liquidating Officer may keep the collections pending its deposit to the bank.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer/collectors/tellers and other accountable officers prepare and submit the individual RAAFs at the end of the month to properly monitor individual accountabilities pursuant to the above-cited provisions.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ensure that the duplicate combinations of the vault are on file with an authorized officer in the Municipal Treasury Office pursuant to the principle of sound internal control provided in Section 123 of P.D. 1445.
We recommended that Reports of Collections & Deposits and its supporting documents be submitted daily to the Municipal Accounting Office so that transactions are promptly recorded and the General Ledgers and Subsidiary Ledgers will be regularly updated to maintain the relevance and value of the financial information.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to apply strict implementation of controls over collections and remittances, and require the collectors of Water System to religiously observe daily remittance of their collections, together with the issued official receipts to avoid misappropriation and/or concealment of irregularities in the handling of cash.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Treasurer to provide at the beginning of the year to the Municipal Accountant the required duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which will be the basis of the latter in booking up the RPT and SET Receivables.  Likewise, the Municipal Accountant shall reconcile the noted difference on both receivable accounts and furnish to the Audit Team results of which.

We reiterated our recommendation that upon review and analysis of the Accounts Payable as at year-end, the Municipal Accountant revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund all undocumented Accounts Payable or those without actual claims that remained outstanding for two years or more.

We reiterated our recommendations:
The Office of the Municipal Treasurer must submit to the Audit Team the List of Delinquent Taxpayers for verification;
The Office of the Municipal Treasurer shall include as additional strategies the conduct of tax campaigns or assemblies in barangays, publishing the Notice of Delinquency in a newspaper of general circulation and posting in social media platforms for information dissemination to constituents; and
Management must maximize the taxing authority mandated under the Local Government Code.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management shall (a) enforce the Contractors to immediately correct the above-noted scaling defects and (b) collect the amounts specifically required to be refunded.
In addition, Management must submit the required Statement of Work Accomplished upon expiry of contract for computation of Liquidated Damages in compliance to Republic Act No. 9184.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor to require Contractor A to refund the computed cost of deficiency by COA Technical Audit Specialist of ₱941,956.88 due to a very serious defect of 68 meters road length shortage upon inspection and structural defect or failure to comply with Sections 62.2.3.1, 62.2.3.2 and 62.2.4 of the RIRR of Republic Act No. 9184.

Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended the following courses of
action:
To the Local Chief Executive:
a) Establish and direct the new Inventory Committee to strictly adhere and comply to
the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006 which prescribe the Guidelines and
Procedures in the Conduct of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(PPE), Recognition of PPE Items Found at Station, and Disposition for Non-
Existing/Missing PPE Items, for the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances
for Government Agencies;
To the Municipal General Services Officer:
b) Conduct an inventory taking in the ensuing years, together with other members of
the Inventory Committee of the LGU and submit the required Report on Physical
Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) every January 31 of each year
to the office of the Auditor;
c) Maintain proper and complete records of the PPEs, particularly the property cards
as mandated under the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I; and
d) Coordinate with the Accounting Office to fast-track the reconciliation of the
inventory reports and accounting records.
To the Municipal Accountant:
e) Regularly maintain and update the PPE ledger cards for each category of assets
as mandated under the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended the following courses of action:
a) To the Local Chief Executive (LCE) to direct the concerned offices to expedite the
implementation of the partially and unimplemented 20% Local Development Fund
programs and projects to fully attain the desired socio-economic development
and environmental management outcomes essential to the promotion of the
general welfare; and
b) To the Local Development Council to identify the cause of the delays or non-
implementation of the PPAs for both current and continuing appropriations and
determine whether the projects are still relevant and aligns with the local
government unit's priorities and needs.

We recommended that the Management expedite the establishment of the Septage
and Sewage Management Ordinance (SSMO) and consequently collaborate with the Water
District, Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH), Department of
Environment and Natural Resources-Environmental Management Bureau (DENR-
EMB), and other relevant stakeholders in the locality to ensure its effective
implementation within its jurisdiction.

1.7. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: (i) prepare
the necessary adjusting entries to remove all tangible items with
acquisition costs below the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00 from
the PPE accounts together with their respective accumulated
depreciation; and (ii) consider the new capitalization threshold under
COA Circular No. 2022-004 in determining whether or not to record newly
acquired tangible items under the PPE accounts.

2.10. We recommended and: (i) The Municipal Budget Officer agreed to
implement internal control measures in the disbursement process, such
as setting deadlines for the submission of claims and only processing
those that are appropriately obligated against their corresponding
budget, which will enable the Municipal Accountant to record the
transactions in a timely manner and prevent the charging of prior year
expenses against the budget of the current year; and (ii) The Municipal
Accountant agreed that prior year expenses that are appropriately
obligated but are not taken up in the books of accounts in the previous
year are recognized in the current year through the Prior Period
Adjustment account rather than through the specific Expenses account.

3.15. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: (i) clearly
document the unverified reconciling items, including their nature,
amount, and any relevant details discovered during the reconciliation, to
support their recording in the accounting books and submit the
corresponding journal entry vouchers to the Audit Team for further
verification; (ii) complete the bank reconciliation statement for the nine
unreconciled bank accounts and provide a copy to the Audit Team; (iii)
include the balances of the dormant accounts totaling ₱236,753.78 in the
year-end balance of “Cash in Bank - Local Currency, Current Account”
account and disclose the status of these accounts in the Notes to
Financial Statements; and (iv) conduct a thorough investigation of the
cause of the negative balances and implement the necessary
adjustments accordingly.

4.9. We recommended that the Local School Board conduct a comprehensive
review of its Special Education Fund budget allocation to align it with
established regulations and prioritized programs as stipulated in Section
272 of R.A. No. 7160, DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017, and
DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2020 and foster transparent
communication and collaboration among entities involved in budgeting
to ensure that financial resources are utilized in a manner consistent with
educational priorities and regulatory requirements.

5.10. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: (i) create an Inventory
Committee whose main priority is to implement the procedures
prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 for the one-time cleansing
of PPE account balances in order to address the issues that are
preventing the reconciliation of records between the concerned offices
and to establish accurate balances of the PPE accounts that are to be
presented in the financial statements; and (ii) augment the manpower of
the General Services Division to enable effective distribution of tasks
and ensure all responsibilities are adequately addressed.

6.11. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to: (a) Demand
immediate liquidation of outstanding cash advances of identified
accountable officers and submit request for write-off of accountability for
the unidentified accounts that existed for 10 years or more as provided in
COA Circular 2023-008; and (b) Ensure that prior cash advances are fully
liquidated before granting a new cash advance to an accountable officer.

7.8. We recommended and the Municipal Engineer agreed to terminate the
subject contract by following the procedures embodied in Item IV under
Annex “I” of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

8.8. We recommended that the MDRRM Officer: (i) Revisit COA Circular No.
2012-002 and prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the provision
stipulated in Item 5.1.2 of the circular. The LDRRMFIP shall be submitted
to the DRRM Council for approval and to be adopted by the local
sanggunian, and this will now be integrated with the Annual Budget,
except for the projects and activities charged to the Special Trust Fund; and
(ii) Furnish the OCD and the DILG-LGOO a copy of the annual LDRRMFIP.

9.7. We recommended that the MDRRM Office and other concerned officials
ensure the timely implementation of the LDRRM identified programs,
projects, and activities, and adopt measures to address the causes in
case of delay or non-implementation thereof.

10.8. We recommended and the Municipal Budget Officer agreed to refrain
from utilizing the budget for the current year to pay prior year
expenditures and only valid obligations booked up as Accounts Payable
shall be processed for payment.

11.9. We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed to stop
the practice of splitting of requisitions and adhere to the provisions of
the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184 in the procurement of meals and
snacks.

12.8. We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee Chairman agreed
to develop a mechanism that would hasten the preparation of contract
documents in order to ensure compliance with the prescribed period of
its submission.

observations.recommendation:
The receipt of funds totaling ₱10,597,800.00 from the DILG-LGSF and the DOH-CHD 1- OCA under the Trust Fund was presented as Share from National Wealth in the Actual Amounts column.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Remove the amount of ₱10,597,800.00 as a Share from National Wealth in the column of Actual Amounts and present this amount as Reconciling Item under basis difference in the Reconciliation between SCBAA and SFPer for the Year Ended December 31, 2022; and


Thereafter, exclude receipts of funds that shall be used for a specific purpose in the Actual Amounts under Revenue of the SCBAA.




The receipt of shares from RA Nos. 7171 and 8240 for CYs 2018, 2019, and 2020 totaling ₱31,212,288.00 received during CY 2022, was presented under the Original Budget column, and appropriation ordinance for FY 2018 and 2019 shares was not prepared.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Correct the presentation of the Original Budgeted Amounts under Economic Services – Current and Continuing Appropriations amounting to ₱39,195,204.05; and


Thereafter, ensure that the amounts presented in the Original Budgeted Amounts are in the Approved Annual Budget.


We also recommended that Management ensure that:

a) all implementations and disbursements on the shares from RA No. 7171 and RA No. 8240 are duly covered by an appropriation ordinance under Section 305(a) of RA No. 7160; and 


b) subsequent legislative enactment of an ordinance shall be undertaken to continue the implementation of the projects, which are MOOE in the following year.

The amount of ₱306,999.48 from CY 2021 CO balances, current and continuing appropriations, and CO from Shares from Tobacco Excise Taxes – FY 2018 & 2019 totaling ₱38,052,287.00 were not presented as CO – Continuing Appropriation in the CY 2022 SCBAA leaving a difference of ₱38,359,286.48.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to: 

Include the amount of ₱306,999.48 from CY 2021 CO balances, current and continuing appropriations, and CO from Shares from Tobacco Excise Taxes – FY 2018 & 2019 totaling ₱38,052,287.00 to be presented in the CY 2022 SCBAA continuing appropriations; and

Thereafter, ensure that all the balances of the prior year’s CO under current and continuing appropriations be included in the current year’s CO – continuing appropriations.




Changes in the Original Budget were not properly presented in the Final Budget Column and were not supported with Appropriation Ordinances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Correct the presentation on the 20% Local Development Fund in the SCBAA by presenting the MOOE and CO with Final Budgeted Amounts amounting to ₱5,100,000.00 and ₱27,100,000.00, respectively, to reflect the correct amount as a result of the reprogramming of the GPPA; and

Thereafter, reflect in the Final Budgeted Amounts the changes made in the Approved Annual Budget as supported by appropriation ordinance.


Under the Special Education Fund (SEF)

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Local School Board to submit the appropriation ordinance/s for the reprogramming/realignment of the SEF to verify the legality of the amounts presented in the SCBAA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to:

Maintain RAAO; and


prepare and submit SAAOB and submit to the Municipal Accountant every month for reconciliation, preparation of SCBAA, and submission to the audit team.
observations.managementAction:
Restated SCBAA of CY 2022 by removing ₱10,597,800.00 representing receipts of Trust Funds from DILG-LGSF and DOH-CHD1 -OCA in the actual amounts of revenue. Rather included in the reconciling item versus Statement of Financial Performance.

Funds with a specific purpose were already excluded by the Municipal Accountant in the SCBAA. 




Corrected presentation in the SCBAA for the implementation of RA7171 and RA8240 amounting to ₱38,052,287.00 CO and Php2,000,000.00 MOOE which was enacted thru resolution in CY 2021.

The approved annual budget was accurately presented in the Original Budgeted Amounts of the CY 2023 SCBAA.  







This is already presented in the Local School Board for the submission of realignment/supplemental budget.



The Budget Office already maintains RAAO which will be the basis in preparing the SAAOB.

Due to time constraint, the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer have not reconciled their respective records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the unrecognized ending inventories as of December 31, 2022, with a cost of at least ₱2,774,809.92 by debiting Drugs and Medicines Inventory-₱1,776,285.92; Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory-₱436,044.00; and Agricultural and Marine Supplies for Distribution-₱562,480.00 and crediting Drugs and Medicines Expenses-₱1,776,285.92; Medical, Dental, and Laboratory Supplies Expenses-₱436,044.00; and Agricultural and Marine Supplies Expenses-₱562,480.00.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Health Officer to identify the unit cost and the total cost of the Drugs and Medicine donated by the other government agencies. Thereafter, furnish a copy to the Municipal Accountant to recognize the “Drugs and Medicines Inventory” in the books of accounts.

We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all the heads of the various offices to submit a list of inventories that remained on hand at year-end to the Municipal Accountant to recognize these items in the books of accounts properly. We further recommended that the Local Chief Executive assign a storage room where the supplies and materials shall be kept upon delivery and monitored by the GSO-Designate.

Restatement of the corresponding inventory accounts in the Financial Statements was applied. Correspondingly, a storage room was already used for office supplies wherein a custodian was assigned to manage its movements.

A reminder inter-office memo from the Accounting Office was circulated in December 2023 to remind Inventory Committee and GSO Office on the need for inventory count to have a basis to recognized ending inventories in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Executive Officer direct the following:

Municipal Treasurer to:

Call the attention or inform the payees of those stale checks by issuing a written notice to surrender the stale checks to him so he could issue new checks according to Section 59 of the MNGAS;

Prepare necessary adjustments in the cashbook on the stale and canceled checks amounting to ₱157,186.21 and ₱140,685.30, respectively, to manifest the correct balance of Cash in Bank, LCCA; and

Submit the canceled checks to the Audit Team through the Municipal Accountant.


Municipal Accountant to:

Restate the CY 2022 financial statements by preparing the following adjusting entries for the stale and canceled checks to reflect the precise account balance of the Cash in Bank, LCCA, and other affected expense accounts. For the stale check, debiting Cash in Bank, LCCA-₱157,186.21; Due to BIR-₱1,036.35 and crediting Due to BIR-₱23,481.01; Accounts Payable- ₱134,741.55. While for the canceled checks, debiting Cash in Bank, LCCA-₱140,685.30; Due to BIR-₱1,730.81 and crediting Other Payables-₱43,960.00; Internet Subscription Expenses-₱18,956.96; Insurance Expense-₱5,049.15; Training Expense-₱17,000.00; Other MOOE-₱18,200.00; Subsidy to NGAs-₱10,000.00; Fidelity Bond Premiums-₱29,250.00; and


Thereafter, ensure that issued checks reported in the RCI are recorded in the books of accounts.

Issued checks are completely recorded in the books.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the MDC to the following:

Ensure that those enumerated lists of GPPAs embodied in the LDIP of the Municipality be of priority to be implemented to guarantee that GPPAs are beneficial to the localities and to ensure that implemented projects have the corresponding budget;

Prepare a well-defined AIP in accordance with the harmonization and synchronization of local planning, investment programming, revenue administration budgeting, and expenditure management prescribed in DILG-NEDA-DBM-DOF Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 series of 2007 dated March 8, 2007 that will make way for the achievement of long-term goals of the Municipality; and

Update the LDIP in case there are changes in the GPPAs to be implemented, which will serve as the basis for preparing and implementing the AIP for the budget year.

Updated the LDIP.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an inspectorate team to assist the Municipal Engineer in monitoring the status of the infrastructure projects.


We, likewise, recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to:

Give precedence to the inspection and supervision of the infrastructure projects of the Municipality;


Carry out detailed engineering investigations, surveys, and designs prior to bidding; and


Report the project status based on actual accomplishment/progress.


Lastly, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that the winning bidder shall indicate the date of receipt of the NTP.
observations.managementAction:
The LCE has created an inspectorate team to assist the Municipal Engineer in monitoring the status of the infrastructure projects.




The engineering office together with the Inspectorate Team consistently inspects and supervises infrastructure projects.

Additional support engineer was hired to carry out detailed investigation and surveys to projects prior to implementation and ensure reports are aligned with actual completion.


Reported the project status based on actual accomplishment/progress.


Date of receipt by the winning bidder in the Notice to Proceed was already indicated.

observations.recommendation: a) Properly implement the LDRRM Plan and optimize utilization of its LDRRM Fund; b) Include in the plans the utilization of the unexpended LDRRM Fund balances from prior years, and specify the list of programs, projects, and activities to be charged against the unexpended LDRRM Fund of previous years in its LDRRM Fund Investment Plan before utilization of the same pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002;
observations.managementAction: AAR 2021 Management agreed to the recommendations.
observations.reason: Approval of the programs were late especially of the unspent balance from previous years.

observations.recommendation: a) To the Local Chief Executive to require the concerned offices/officials to identify the gaps in the determination and implementation of development projects thereby come up with corrective measures to ensure optimal utilization of funds and timely delivery of services; b) To the Municipal Engineering Office to submit status report of the continuing programs, projects and activities including plans and action for pending/unimplemented programs.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2021 Management agreed to the recommendations and actions to improve utilization and reporting.

observations.recommendation: a) Require Heads of departments/offices to ensure that GAD projects, activities and programs are implemented as planned to attain GAD objectives; b) Strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 for timely submission of reports; c) Maximize utilization of GAD funds through implementation of GAD-related programs and projects; d) Require GAD Focal Point System to institutionalize GAD database and monitoring and evaluation system.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 & 2021 Management agreed and committed to improve GAD planning, implementation and reporting.

observations.recommendation: a) Reassess and reconsider the internal controls, policies and procedures adopted by the Municipality in recognizing, monitoring, safeguarding and reporting of its Property, Plant and Equipment; b) Intensify the institutionalization of an Inventory Committee to conduct actual physical inventory of its PPE annually and establish required procedures in Planning, Execution, and Reconciliation of GSO and Accounting records; c) Instruct Municipal Accountant to maintain and update PPE ledger cards and GSO property cards; d) Ensure insurable physical assets are adequately protected; e) Require Municipal Accountant to recognize all completed Road Network Assets on cost or fair value and direct relevant offices to perform duties in accounting and reporting of Local Road Network.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 & 2021 Recorded/ liquidation required does not fall on a year-end thus could not update year-end balances. The function is too technical, and no available manpower to do the inventory.
observations.reason: Recorded/ liquidation required does not fall on a year-end thus could not update year-end balances; Absence of manpower to focus on inventories.

observations.recommendation: a) Require responsible offices to maintain stock cards and properly monitor items to generate a more reliable inventory report; b) Ensure issued items be supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and submit to Municipal Accountant within five days after end of month and submit liquidation documents; c) Municipal Accountant to institute adjustments and maintain inventory ledger cards and promptly update; d) Review and reassess accounting policy and procedure for inventory.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2020 Recorded/ restocking listing does not fall on a year-end thus could not update the year-end balances. Already - Absence of supporting documents to liquidate the consumable items. Lack of manpower to focus on inventories.
observations.reason: Restocking does not fall on year-end thus could not update year-end balances; Absence of supporting documents to liquidate consumable items; Lack of manpower.

Likewise, agreed to submit lacking supporting documents for compliance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant began reconciling the balances in the affected accounts.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant began reconciling the balances in the affected accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the above provisions to maintain a separate account for trust fund and directly deposits in the trust fund account. Further, agreed to maintain a separate bank account for DRRM Funds and transfer all cash donations for DRRM to this account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

fast track the implementation of priority programs and projects for the benefits of its citizens. In the event that there was physical but no financial accomplishment, this should be disclosed in the accomplishment report of Management for validation.
observations.reason: Reiterated in Observation No. 9, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Municipal Focal Point System for addressing GAD issues and concerns of the Municipality. But, still, we recommended that the Focal Point System establish the Municipal GAD data base in order to identify more gender issues and concerns that need to be addressed.
observations.managementAction: GAD database will be established after the 2022 Community-Based Monitoring System.
observations.reason: The MPDC admitted that GAD database is not yet established. However, a province-wide update on Community-Based Monitoring System (CBMS) which will be one of the bases of the formulation of the GAD database will be conducted by the PGA.

observations.managementAction:
Municipal Budget Officer to trace the said transactions in CY 2020 AIP and Municipal Accountant will adjust all affected accounts in the preparation of the Comparative Financial Statements of the incoming year and to refer with Annex B – Local Government Chart of Accounts in classifying transactions.

Municipal Accountant adjusted the affected accounts accordingly after determining appropriate classification of the transactions.



Municipal Budget strictly requires completeness of information in Obligation Requests from requesting offices/departments otherwise, he will not further process such request.
observations.reason: The said unclassified transactions are yet to be traced by the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant.

observations.managementAction:
Collectors remits intact and daily collections of the day to the liquidating officer, who will prepare the Report of Collections and Deposits thereafter and will turn it over with the total receipt of the day to the Municipal Treasurer.





Replenishment of the petty cash fund will only be made through checks and to request from the Sangguniang Bayan to create special cash fund for AICS.
observations.reason: Remitted collections were net of disbursements paid through cash collections. Reiterated in this Report.

observations.recommendation:
Instruct all end-users or requesting office to religiously coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer as to the source of funds of the programs/projects/activities being implemented.

Enjoin the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conscientiously refer to the approved annual investment plan as part of their detective control before further processing the request, issuing the check to the end users, and providing advice for encashment.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to trace the unreconciled inter-fund transactions totaling ₱879,328.99 through prior years’ journals, ledgers, disbursement vouchers through the assistance with the Municipal Treasurer in the determination of the correct balances of the accounts. 

Thereafter, require them to immediately transfer all inter-fund receivables, especially those already identified totaling ₱5,477,552.59, to their appropriate fund bank accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Municipal Budget Officer strictly required completeness of information in the Obligation Requests, otherwise, postpone further processing of the said requests.



Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer referred with the approved annual investment before processing and paying transactions of the Municipality to avoid payment through inappropriate funds.



Accounting Office to exert their best efforts to trace the unreconciled balance of ₱879, 328.99 of the inter-fund accounts.





Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer immediately transferred the amount of ₱5,447,552.59 to their appropriate fund bank accounts.
observations.reason: On-going reconciliation of the unreconciled balance.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Bids and Awards Committee and Technical Working Group to limit claims of honorarium for successfully completed procurements under competitive bidding to the fixed rate per number of procured project as provided in DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005.

In the event that the sale of bids is not enough to cover the BAC Honoraria, direct the BAC to request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan allowing them to utilize savings from project for their Honoraria.
observations.managementAction:
BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG to claim only honorarium up to the maximum allowable amount as prescribed by Item 5.3 of DBM BC No. 2004-5A.




The BAC to request authority from the Sangguniang Bayan allowing them to utilize savings from project for their Honoraria in case that sale of bids is not enough to cover the BAC Honoraria.
observations.reason: Post audit of transactions revealed that claims of BAC honoraria were still based on a fixed rate of their monthly salary.

observations.recommendation:
Submit justifications to support awarding of the listed projects despite existence of negative slippage on the on-going projects declared by the winning bidders in their Statement of All On-Going Government and Private Contracts.

Ensure that all vital information in the Statement of All On-Going Government and Private Contracts are completely provided such as contract duration, date started and percentage of completion, otherwise disqualify bidders who are not complying with the said requirements. 

Conduct in-depth post qualification of submitted technical requirements of bidders by examining on-going projects if there is a negative slippage as well as conduct ocular inspections on those declared projects to evaluate the ability of the prospective winning bidder to complete project under bid with quality standards and scheduled time frame set.

Since the certificate of final acceptance is yet to be issued, require all contractors to give performance bond for the remaining uncovered period of the completed projects, otherwise, suspend issuance of the certificate until complied with. Thereafter, require performance bond from future awardees covering the required period of validity by Section 39.4 of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction:
BAC to provide justification to support awarding of the projects to the winning bidders.




The BAC to provide the necessary details in the Statement of All On-Going Government and Private Contracts.
observations.reason: Justifications were not obtained from those with identified negative slippage since projects were already completed on time, in full and within quality standards.

observations.managementAction:
BAC to submit BAC Resolution recommending award of the contract to the supplier and Notice of Award



BAC to require Income Tax Return and the complete Omnibus Sworn Statement of the suppliers for all procured goods awarded with an ABC above ₱500,000.00.

BAC to ensure that the procurement method to be applied will be consistent from the Annual Procurement Plan up to the bidding documents to avoid confusion. 

Further, BAC to require and submit lacking documents from the supplier of the completed transactions and ensure that documentary requirements are complete before further processing transactions.
observations.reason: BAC Resolution recommending award of the contract to the supplier and Notice of Award are yet to be submitted.

observations.recommendation:
Require from the identified taxpayers additional payment of real property taxes; and 

Implement tax discount and penalty rates consistent with Provincial Ordinance No. 115-2017 and other issuances of the Province of La Union pertaining to the imposition and collection of real property taxes.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Treasurer to harmonize the grant of discount and penalty rates for the collection of Real Property Tax and to collect from the identified taxpayers the additional Real Property Taxes amounting ₱241,672.63 in the incoming year.
observations.reason: Additional tax due is yet to be collected from the taxpayers.

observations.recommendation:
The General Services Officer to prepare and maintain Property Cards of the different PPEs.

Inventory Committee to conduct inventory of PPEs maintained by the different department/offices of the Municipality.

Municipal Accountant to revisit previous records for local road networks prior to CY 2016.

Municipal Accountant to prepare subsidiary ledgers for the different components of the local road networks.

GSO-Designate with the Inventory Committee to finalize the inventory of local roads including the inventory of road components using the prescribed formats, Annexes A and C of COA Circular No. 2015-008.

Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

General Services Officer to prepare and maintain Property Cards of the different PPEs.

Inventory Committee to conduct inventory of PPEs maintained by the different department/offices of the Municipality.





Municipal Accountant to revisit previous records for local road networks prior to CY 2016.

Management prioritized provision of relief to affected families of the Covid-19 pandemic.

Property Cards were not yet prepared by the GSO due to voluminous work.

The Municipal Treasurer to record transactions in the cashbook on a monthly basis.

Municipal Accountant to reconcile records with the Cashbook.

observations.reason: Discrepancies still remain in the books.
