The different department/offices to prepare their PPMPs in accordance with Section 7 of RA 9184 using the suggested format by the GPPB.



The Municipal Budget Officer to review and evaluate the PPMPs in terms of amount and its coherence with the proposed budget.

Management to prepare the APP in accordance with the suggested format
observations.reason:
While PPMPs of the different department were prepared with regard to the Annual Budget, supplemental PPMPs were not prepared for reprogrammed projects, programs and activities.

Not implemented.
There was no PPMPs prepared for supplemental budgets.

observations.recommendation: Automatically release the Special Education Tax Income to the SEF and Timely recognize in the cashbooks and collections and deposits
observations.managementAction: Management to timely transfer SET income to the Special Education Fund.
observations.reason: Review of records showed that transfer of SET income from the local treasury was only transferred on a monthly basis instead of date of remittance from liquidating officer to the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
Require additional payment for the uncollected penalties during the CY 2019. Thereafter, strictly implement with the penalty provisions of the current Tax Ordinance, requiring assessment and collection of penalties for delayed payments of business taxes as additional income or funds for projects to be implemented as well as ensure earlier collection of the said funds; and

Require the certified copy of Annual Income Tax Return to Business Taxpayers on the second quarter of the year and adjust the business tax and collect the difference if there are discrepancies between the amount appearing in the ITR and gross sales declared in the business tax application form.
observations.managementAction:
Management to collect the additional uncollected penalties.



Management required the submission of certified copy of Annual Income Tax Returns from Business Taxpayers in contrast with their existing tax ordinance.
observations.reason: Additional uncollected penalties are yet to be collected from taxpayers.

observations.recommendation:
Strategically schedule GAD projects and vigilantly monitor its implementation so that its purpose will be actually realized and enjoyed by majority of the community during the year.

Update their GAD Database with latest necessary data required in Annex B of JMC 2013-001 to have a more reliable basis in identifying priority gender issues to be addressed for the coming year. Thereafter, judiciously conduct gender analysis using the updated GAD Database in identifying priority gender issues and formulating high-impact programs/projects/activities to address such during planning and budgeting of GAD Funds.
observations.managementAction:
Management to implement programs and activities under GAD as scheduled.



Management updated the GAD Database.
observations.reason: Some programs/projects/activities were not yet implemented as scheduled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to provide the necessary economic useful life for the property items identified in the above table and compute the depreciation expense.  Draw JEV, for any adjustments, and furnish a copy to the Audit Team for reference.
observations.managementAction: The initial data prepared for the Road Network Inventory has no details on estimated useful life, hence no depreciation was recognized. The management however commits to ensure that Economic Useful Life of the asset be provided to compute the Depreciation Expense to be recognized in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

o submit the eight (8) enumerated lacking documents as basis in ascertaining completeness and authenticity of the transactions; and

o for future similar transactions, ensure completeness of documentation before effecting payment on all transactions, to comply with the requirements of pertinent rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management commits to submit the required documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management –

o require the Engineering Office, the BAC and Accounting Office to submit the duly approved plans and drawings, program of works including statement of work accomplished as basis in the conduct of technical evaluation; and

o instruct the Accounting Office to ensure the completeness of supporting documents of transactions in accordance with the provisions of Section 4 (5) and (6) of P.D. No. 1445 prior to disbursement of funds.
observations.managementAction: The Management commits to submit the required documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

o submit proofs of receipt/distribution of these procured goods to various beneficiaries; 

o submit the lacking documents specified in the table as basis in ascertaining completeness and authenticity of the transactions; and

o for future similar transactions, ensure completeness of documentation before effecting payment on all transactions, to comply with the requirements of pertinent rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management commits to submit the required documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management- 

a. require the personnel in-charge in documenting transactions to immediately submit the lacking documents enumerated in the table as basis in the conduct of evaluation.

b. prepare work program and statement of work accomplished in order to monitor its physical against financial accomplishments and to substantiate the expenditures for fuel used by various equipment in the implementation of various projects and wages of laborers assigned in river control management.

c. strictly adhere with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in the preparation of Annual Investment Plan to ensure that all items to be funded are development programs/projects and activities that partakes the nature of investment or capital expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The Management commits to submit the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality of Luna through the Municipal Accountant, recognize/ take up in the books the transferred properties to correctly state the balance of the Property, Plant and Equipment and the Equity Account amounting to ₱14,581,147.06.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
exert effort in determining the claimants/creditors of the ₱2,865,473.74 recorded as Other Payables to ensure its validity and existence;
make the necessary adjustments in the books to reclassify the Other Payables amounting to ₱589,532.73 and account as credit to the Government Equity account considering that it was supposed to be an income in CY 2019; and
reclassify the Other Payables with negative balances totaling ₱185,934.99 as Receivables and enforce collection thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to adhere to pertinent provisions of existing/appropriate laws, rules and regulations in granting allowances and other benefits to its officials and employees to avoid overpayment resulting to audit disallowances.

We recommended that management give notice to the contractor of his delayed prosecution of the project and inform him of its consequences like the payment of liquidated damages, forfeiture of performance security, rescission of contract and such other sanctions applicable. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer conduct an assessment of the accomplishment of the contractor as of the targeted completion to determine the value of unperformed work as of that date which should be the basis in computing liquidated damages.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the submission of an irrevocable letter of credit of equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee or a surety bond callable on demand. In case the contractor has a pending billing, the whole amount of the advance payment should be recouped at once.

We recommended that Management, thru the OBO, ensure that no building/structure shall be constructed within the Municipality without first obtaining a building permit to ensure their conformity with  approved standard requirements on zoning and land use, lines and grades, structural design, sanitary and sewerage, environmental health, electrical and mechanical safety as well as with other rules and regulations promulgated in accordance with the PD 1096 aside from generating additional income for the Municipality.

We recommended that Management cause the submission of CY 2021 required reports with supporting documents and/or schedules not yet submitted. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 347 of RA No. 7160 to ensure timely submission of accounts, financial statements and other required reports to the office of the auditor.

We recommended and Management agreed, thru GFPS, to ensure that full implementation of GAD PPAs is achieved to promote empowerment of women and pursue equal opportunities for women and men and to ensure equal access to resources and to development results and outcome.

We recommended that:
Management, through the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, hasten the implementation of development programs and projects for the benefits of its citizens; and
revisit projects funded in CYs 2017 to 2020 and determine if these are still implementable, otherwise, consider their reversion and re-appropriation to more priority development programs and projects.
We recommended that Management require the concerned officials and employees to settle promptly their suspensions and disallowance

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) request from the Sangguniang Bayan Members for a Resolution authorizing her to donate the said PPEs to farmers’ association.
Thereafter, we recommended that Management prepare deeds of donation for the actual transfer of ownership to farmers’ association on the said PPEs and cancel the Property Acknowledgment Receipt issued to the Municipal Agriculturist.
Lastly, we recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the donated PPEs in their books with the following entries:
Accumulated Depreciation- 		         ₱125,654.14
Other Machinery and Equipment
Accumulated Depreciation- 		 35,550.00
Other Machinery and Equipment
Government Equity 		        1,442,642.98
Construction of Heavy Equipment 		      ₱1,185,000.00
Other Machinery and Equipment		            418,847.12
The Management agreed with all our recommendations and assured the Audit Team that a deed of donation or resolution be prepared, require the acknowledgement of the beneficiaries for the donated PPE and consequently for the Municipal Accountant to derecognize the donated PPE in their books of account. Also, to cancel the PAR issued to the Municipal Agriculture Officer.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Budget Officer and Office of the Sanggunian Bayan to restraint from allocating and approving legislative budgets for PPAs other than for general administrative purposes pursuant to Sections 445 and 447 of the RA 7160.
The Municipal Budget Officer and Office of the Sanggunian Bayan guaranteed that legislative budgets would only be allocated for general administrative purposes.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
HRMO, Municipal Accountant, and Acting Municipal Treasurer to formulate internal policies and procedures on the evaluation and approval of salary loan applications of employees; and submit to the LCE for her approval. Thereafter, implement the approved policies and procedures and encourage employees to adhere thereon;
Payroll Officer/ERF Handler, through the Municipal Accountant, to confer with GSIS on the noted underpayment of loans; and
Payroll Officer to exercise diligence in monitoring net take home pay of Municipal personnel to comply with the requirements of existing law.
During our exit conference, Management agreed with our recommendations and stated that they had already started implementing the recommendations this June 2022.

We recommended that the LCE direct the official concerned to submit additional documentary requirements such as case study reports and travel orders/locators when she was assigned to hazardous areas for all the time when she claimed hazard pay.
Likewise, we recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to desist from paying hazard pay and subsistence allowance unless the public social worker concerned is exposed to actual hazard and is required to make service available at all times including break-times.
With the foregoing recommendations, the Management agreed to direct the concerned official to submit the additional documentary requirements. Moreover, to pay hazard pay and subsistence allowance in accordance with the guidelines set forth under the abovementioned circulars.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to issue official receipt for the remaining balance of the donation amounting to ₱58,973.50 and subsequently deposit the same in the appropriate bank account and;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to adhere with the accounting and reporting guidelines of donations required under Section 5.1.12 of Item V and Annex C of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Item VII(2)(c) of COA Circular No. 2014-002.
Thereafter, ensure that future donations to be given to the Municipality will be authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan and; acknowledged, recorded and accounted for in congruence with the above-cited provisions.
During our exit conference, the Municipal Treasurer assured that official receipt be issued for the remaining balance of the cash donation while the Municipal Accountant guaranteed that accounting and reporting guidelines for donations be adhered to in case donations will be given again to them.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
Local Finance Committee to appropriate certain amount for the repair of the ambulance so that it will not remain idle. However, file appropriate charges against the concerned employees;
GSO – Designate to renew all insurable properties especially the Municipality’s vehicles and keep the copies of the insurance policies; monitor their expirations; and
Municipal Accountant to record the insurance based on accrual method of accounting and prepare a schedule of the insurance policies that will serve as a monitoring tool to determine which insurance policies are nearing their expirations.
The Management intends to have a meeting with the incoming administration to talk about the allocation of a certain amount for the repair of the ambulance. Further, the GSO-Designate agreed with our recommendation and plans to renew all insurable properties of the Municipality while the Municipal Accountant to record insurance based on accrual method of accounting and prepare a schedule of insurance policies.

We recommended that the LCE ensure that rules and regulations on the proper handling of collections and disbursement of government funds are being practiced by the Municipality. Likewise, we recommended that the LCE direct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to:
a.	Compel all collectors to remit their collections daily and intact to him and deposit all remittances on the same or next banking day; and
b.	Use the established petty cash fund to pay AICS and various petty expenses.
The OIC-Municipal Treasurer positively took note of our recommendation and stated that collections were remitted to him daily and intact. Further, petty cash fund is used for the payment of AICS and various petty expenses.

We recommended that the LCE:
instruct the GSO-Designate and BAC Secretariat to submit copies of Purchase Orders, supporting documents, delivery receipts and, AIR to the Auditor within the prescribed period; and
direct the GSO-Designate to:
furnish the audit team with notices of deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance of delivered goods; and
check the purchased goods upon delivery prior to acceptance to ensure the completeness and conformity of the delivered goods with the specifications indicated in the Purchase Orders as he is the one accountable for the properties of the Municipality.
The Management will comply with the recommendations and committed to submit contracts/purchase orders and notice of deliveries within the prescribed period. Also, for the GSO-Designate to check goods upon delivery prior to acceptance.

We recommended that the Management ensure that all disbursements of funds are duly covered by an appropriation ordinance in compliance with Section 305(a) of RA No. 7160. Thereafter, pass an appropriation ordinance, not just a resolution, to authorize the use and disbursement of shares from collection of excise taxes under RA No. 7171 and RA No. 8240.
During our exit conference, the Management assured that appropriation ordinance be enacted before the use and disbursement of RA 7171 and 8240.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Direct the Agricultural Officer to conduct consultation with tobacco farmers to determine programs/projects/activities beneficial for them to increase their productivity, enhance quality of products and increase their income.
Confer with the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution identifying the list of programs and projects to be implemented from the remaining share of the Municipality from the FY 2015 collection of excise tax from locally manufactured virginia-type cigarettes covered under LBM No. 76-A amounting to ₱848,651.00.
Subsequently, thru the Municipal Budget Officer, request from the DBM the release of said remaining share by submitting the required documents as provided for in Item 2.5 of LBM 76.
The Municipal Budget Officer noted our recommendation and committed to visit the DBM Office to verify their remaining shares. Accordingly, the Agricultural Officer to consult with tobacco farmers to determine PPAs to be funded from their remaining shares and request from the Sangguniang Bayan to pass a resolution to authorize the use of their remaining share from FY 2015 collection of excise tax.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Councils to judiciously plan the use of the unexpended balances of the LDRRMF from previous years and ensure that the same are incorporated in the LDRRMFIP of the Municipality. Likewise, to review and update annually the LDRRMFIP and if changes is to be made in the listed PPAs, submit a revised LDRRMFIP as approved by the local sanggunian and Local Chief Executive (LCE) upon recommendation of the LDRRMC. Thereafter, pass a resolution/appropriation ordinance authorizing the use of such fund for certain PPAs. Further, include in the APP or its supplemental all PPAs to be implemented charged under STF-LDRRMF.
The Management agreed with our recommendations and guaranteed that the use of unexpended balances of the LDRRMF be supported with a resolution/appropriation ordinance and PPAs to be charged under STF be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Engineer/MPDC-Designate to assist the barangay in establishing WSP or BAWASAs and set policies for the operation and maintenance of the SALINTUBIG project subject for review and approval by the Sangguniang Bayan.
The Municipal Engineer stated that he will confer with the beneficiaries of the SALINTUBIG projects to assist them in establishing WSP or BAWASAs.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Engineer to adhere with the provisions and guidelines set-forth under the DO No. 197 of the DPWH series of 2016 for the proper preparation of the ABC.
We also recommended that the LCE instruct the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to collect the excess payment made to the contractors.
From the foregoing recommendations, the Municipal Engineer agreed to comply with the provisions and guidelines set-forth on the abovementioned department order for the proper preparation of ABC. Further, the TWG to extensively verify the BOQ. Also, the OIC-Municipal Treasurer committed to contact the contractors to collect the excess amount.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Engineer to:
Undertake necessary detailed engineering prior to procurement of infrastructure projects;
Require the contractor to repair the defects noted during the inspection and submit warranty security against Structural Defects and Failures for the remaining period; and
Thereafter, require contractors whose projects are already one year completed to post, prior to issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance, warranty against structural defects from final acceptance of the project up to the period prescribed in Section 62.2.3.2.
The Municipal Engineer agreed to comply with our recommendations and assured that detailed engineering be undertaken prior to procurement of infrastructure projects. In addition, to require the contractor to repair the defects noted during the inspection and submit warranty security against structural defects and failures for the remaining period. Moreover, to require contractors to post warranty security for projects that were one yar completed.

We recommended that the LCE direct the BPLO and Municipal Treasurer to review all the contracts of lease entered into by the Municipality and the stall owners. Likewise, amend or renew, as much as possible, the expired contract with the lessee occupying the leased premises of the municipality pursuant to provisions provided in the 2017 Municipal Revenue Code.
Likewise, issue demand notices for the collection of unpaid monthly rental to all the lessees who are in default. In case the lessor failed to pay the said stall rentals for a period of three months, the lessor shall cancel or revoke the contract.
We commended Management for installing ATM Payroll System which is a sound system of internal control and same must be fully complied and sustained for CY 2022 onwards.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall exercise prudence and care in recording and accounting financial transactions to ensure that balances of reciprocal accounts are reconciled. Likewise, undertake regular and timely review and reconciliation of the accounts in compliance to the above-mentioned rules and regulations.
We recommended that Management submit legal basis to support its entering into the afore-quoted part of the Deed of Absolute Sale and Transfer.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to provide detailed additional information on accounts that are not presented on the face of the statement of financial position, statement of financial performance, statement of changes in net assets/equity or cash flow statement, but that is relevant to an understanding of any of them

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review and analyze the age of the Due to NGAs account to be able to identify funds that remained idle in the LGU books. Further, any unused/unutilized balances of completed projects shall be immediately returned to the Source Agencies and/or remitted to the National Treasury as required under COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended the following corrective actions:
The Municipal Accountant to review and correct the previous year’s balance and record the same together with disclosed CY 2021 unexpended balance as Trust Liabilities-DRRM in the Trust Fund books; and
Upon setting-up the correct balance, the Municipal Treasurer shall transfer the corresponding cash back-up of the Trust Liabilities-DRRM in the Trust Fund Regular account in line with Section 5.1.10 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 to properly restrict its funding requirement.
We recommended management to adhere strictly with the prescribed period of remittances to the proper recipient government agencies in compliance with the applicable laws and rules.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to set-up the correct balance of 5% Calamity Fund as at December 31, 2021 to Trust Liability-DRRM account in the Trust Fund books;
Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to submit the ordinance and supplemental plans, if any, to support changes in the budget; and
Strictly and prudently comply with all the rules and regulations laid out relative to LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that Management shall enjoin the concerned officials to strictly comply with the reportorial requirements laid out in COA Circular No. 2012-002 to have a more reliable and accurate monitoring report for 5% LDRRM Fund utilization and balances.

We recommended Management to:
Prioritize the augmentation of the mandatory positions of the LDRRMO through transfer of existing vacant positions from other offices/units and appropriate sufficient funds thereof; and
Ensure that the minimum standards and qualifications for the mandatory positions required under JMC 2014-1 are fully complied.

We recommended that Management:
Require the concerned officials to submit original copies of proofs of distribution, with identification of the type of relief goods, duly signed by actual beneficiaries for our evaluation; and
Enjoin all concerned officials to strictly comply with the above-stated law, rules and regulations.

We recommended Management to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the “Other Payables” and “Trust Liabilities” accounts; and
Withhold taxes on payments of BAC honoraria and ensure remittance to BIR within the prescribed period.

We recommended that Management must comply with the rule that payments shall be made by checks directly payable to the suppliers and streamlined process of procurement mandated in Republic Act No. 9184 must be observed.

We recommended Management to limit the use of Petty Cash Fund for non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses and must strictly comply with the applicable rules and regulations under PD 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to instruct the OIC-General Services Officer to prepare property cards as required by Sections 114 and 119 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs in order to conduct reconciliation of the property and accounting records to provide accurate amount for the PPE account.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to enjoin the Inventory Committee and all concerned offices to:
Undertake the inventory taking in accordance with the procedures set in the new Guidelines; and
Reconcile and update their records and reports accordingly.

We recommended that Management shall fully comply with the posting requirements laid-out in the above-mentioned law, rules and regulations in order to fully promote transparency, accountability, and good governance policies and measures in the procurement process.

We recommended that Management stop charging ineligible expenses against the 20% Development Fund and ensure that the appropriation of project expenditures and/or utilization of Funds are strictly in accordance with the DILG-DBM-Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

We recommended that the Management submit legal basis for the increase in the Pag-IBIG government compulsory contribution which requires the proper consultation and approval of DBM as required under RA 11640 and RA 9679. The management should strictly observe the maximum limit of PAG-IBIG government compulsory contribution at P100.00 per month per official/employee unless the LGU consult with and secure approval of DBM.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person timely submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report with the required supporting documents cited in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 and pursuant to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001.

We recommend that Management:

direct the Inventory Committee to hasten the conduct of physical inventory and submit to the Audit Team a copy of the RPCI and RPCPPE; and




require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards or Property Cards and Ledger Cards and to reconciliation of their records regularly.
observations.managementAction: RPCI for the semesters ended June 30, 2021 and December 31. 2021 were submitted on September 17, 2021 and March 22, 2022, respectively, and RPCPPE for the year ended December 31, 2021 was submitted on March 22, 2022.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee is yet to furnish the person responsible for a copy of the RCPI and RPCPPE which will serve as the basis for the updating of Stock Cards and Property Cards. Reports were submitted to the Audit Team but were not a result of physical inventory taking rather a mere consolidation of reports from different departments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management, thru the Municipal Accountant:

effect the following adjusting entry in the Trust Fund Books to correct the balances of the affected accounts:

record/close the CIP account of completed projects to the appropriate asset account and transfer the same to the General Fund books.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant effect adjustments to reconcile the understatement of Cash in Bank balance amounting to ₱490,051.62 as of December 31, 2020. Henceforth, prepare monthly BRS and submit the same to the Audit Team in accordance to the provisions of Item 3.2 of COA Circular No.  96-011 dated October 2, 1996.
observations.reason: The CIB balance were properly reconciled, however, BRS for CY 2021 were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the corresponding distribution sheets and RSMI to the Audit Team. Henceforth, the Accounting Unit shall initially record goods purchased and held for distribution to their appropriate inventory accounts and recognize expenses only upon distribution to end-users as evidenced by appropriate reports such as, but not limited to, distribution sheets and RSMI.
observations.managementAction: The distribution sheets related to COVID 19 relief goods have been submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management effect the following proposed adjusting journal entries to reclassify expenses to PPE accounts and take up the corresponding accumulated depreciation for the year:

We recommended that the Accountant take up the completed building to its proper asset account, Building and Other Structures, and correspondingly close its Construction in Progress – Building and Other Structures account in the books.
We recommended that Management adhere with the provision of LBC No. 125 in identifying PPAs and expenses that could be charged against the BGCM.

We recommended that Management thru the LDRRMC monitor the implementation of PPAs chargeable against the LDRRMF to ensure the Municipality’s disaster resiliency and preparedness and strengthen its disaster response capabilities.

We recommended that Management submit the corresponding distribution sheets and RSMIs to support the recording of expense totaling ₱3,072,932.25. Henceforth, ensure that all transactions are supported with complete documentation.
observations.managementAction: The distribution sheets related to the relief goods were already submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Health Officer (MHO) and the BAC to consider the DOH DPRI and DOH DMs in the preparation of purchase requests for the procurement of medicines and medical supplies.
observations.managementAction: Purchases of various medicines and supplies in 2021 were already based on DOH DPRI and DOH DMs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, thru the GSO, strictly adhere to the terms of delivery indicated in the contracts to avoid over-stocking which could expose the items, especially medicines, to risk of possible expiration and wastage.
observations.managementAction:
All suppliers of goods adhered to the delivery terms as indicated in the contracts in 2021.

It was also made sure that the expiry date of drugs and medicines is beyond one (1) year from the date of delivery.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management include as a client-focused gender project and incorporate in their budget the repairs and maintenance of abovementioned comfort rooms for the benefit of their tenants, clients and the general public.
observations.managementAction: As of June 22, 2022, NTP has been issued for the repair and maintenance of comfort rooms in various buildings and stalls owned by the LGU.
observations.reason: Repair and maintenance of comfort rooms in various buildings and stalls owned by the LGU is still on going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to be done by the concerned employees of the Municipality:

That the Disposal Committee act on the disposal of the unserviceable Mitsubishi Strada Vehicle of the Municipality;
observations.managementAction: The Disposal Committee has initiated the public auction of the unserviceable Mitsubishi Strada on September 2020. However, no bidder has expressed interest on the said unit. Management plans to repost the invitation to bid along with other unserviceable equipment of the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Disposal Committee has taken the necessary actions for the disposal of the said unit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Assessor and the GSO Designate facilitate the titling of the parcels of land in the name of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
The LGU initiated the titling of lots in 2020, however, the COVID 19 pandemic stalled the processing of these land titles.

As of today, the LGU only managed to secure two (2) titles of land:
7,231.00 SQM LOT (Municipal Hall Grounds in Poblacion), and
539.00 SQM LOT (Rural Health Unit in Turod)

A land survey is currently ongoing for the other lots owned by the municipality.
observations.reason: Some titles have already been transferred under the name of the municipal government but titles are yet to be claimed from the Registry of Deeds. On the other hand, the Municipal Assessor is in the process of securing documentary requirements for the titling of the other parcels of land.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management collect the un-withheld aggregate taxes from the concerned contractors. Or, if the same were already paid, require the presentation of the corresponding official receipts.

Henceforth, Management, thru the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant impose the withholding of aggregate taxes or the presentation of official receipts, if already paid.

We recommended that Management, thru the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Accountant, identify the road network where the completed project belongs and make the necessary adjustments in the book of accounts of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Road Networks account presented in the financial statements only includes the cost of the road lot and concrete pavement based on the Municipal Street Map from MPDC.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management conduct an inventory of local roads under its jurisdiction and properly record in the books of accounts of the LGU.

We also recommended that Management enjoin the following department heads for the compliance with the requirements of COA Circular No. 2015-008:




Municipal General Services Officer

Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and


Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components. (Annex E of the Circular)


Municipal Engineer

Provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction: The management maintains the Local Road Network Ledger Card summarized in the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network and supported by the Municipal Street Map.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, thru the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer exert diligent efforts to locate the supporting documents necessary to establish the existence and validity of the receivable accounts and exhaust all means to cause their collection, otherwise a request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts from the Commission on Audit shall be initiated.
observations.reason: According to Management, a letter has been sent to PGA. However, a duly received copy of which and PGO’s written response, if any, is yet to be received by the Team.  Also, Management has committed to demand payment from various individual debtors and NGOs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, thru the SWMB:


Re-establish the collection of recyclable wastes thru the point-system to encourage proper waste disposal; and,
observations.reason: Management has re-established the point-system. However, due to the bulk of the collected and shredded recyclable wastes, collection of the same was again deferred.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, thru the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Assessor hasten the cleansing of real property taxpayer data records to enable Management to identify appropriate action in the collection of these delinquent RPTs.
observations.managementAction:
Cleansing taxpayer data records is arduous due to duplication of tax declarations. The Municipal Assessor cannot cancel the declaration without the authority/waiver of the owners. Properties in San Gregorio and Turod were covered by the town site under Presidential Decree (PD) No. 1088 dated February 7, 1977. These properties should have not incurred taxes and penalties after PD1088.

Personnel from the Treasury Office cannot locate the declared owners.

The Municipal Assessor and Acting Municipal Treasurer commits to exert more effort in cleansing taxpayer’s data records.
observations.reason: Cleansing of real property taxpayer data records is still on process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management submit the disbursement vouchers completely supported with the required documents.

We recommended that Management:

monitor cash advances to avoid accumulation; and
observations.reason: Liquidations of cash advances granted to officers and employees in CY 2021 were promptly submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management through the BAC stop procuring goods out-rightly through direct contracting and emergency purchase; rather, consider widening its pool of suppliers to obtain the best deal for the government as required under the Procurement Act.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2020, some supplies and materials were already purchased thru public bidding. However, some were still procured out-rightly thru direct contracting and emergency purchase.
Management commits to look into the matter and collect/impose the said amount from the contractor.
observations.reason: The liquidated damages were collected on April 1, 2022 as evidenced by OR No. 1647023.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

demand the concerned beneficiaries to return the equipment and materials intended for their livelihood projects; and




monitor regularly the implementation of the projects to ensure compliance with the terms and conditions of the Agreement.
observations.managementAction:
The management finds it impossible to demand the return of various equipment from the Apayao Vendors Association, aside from a two-door refrigerator, as these pieces of equipment were already lost or damaged.

The original members of the association who signed in the memorandum of agreement between DOLE and Apayao Vendors Association are no longer part of the association.


The original members of the association who signed in the memorandum of agreement between DOLE and Apayao Vendors Association are no longer part of the association.
observations.reason: As discussed during the Exit Conference, only the refrigerator  has been recalled and was issued to another member of the same association. A copy of the Property Acknowledgement Receipt shall be submitted to the Team.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) adopt necessary measures to ensure compliance by the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to existing laws and regulations for the timely submission of accounts or reports of the LGU’s transactions, otherwise, sanctions maybe instituted, if warranted.
observations.reason: Financial transaction documents for CY 2021 were submitted to the Team but beyond the recommended period.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management furnish the COA auditor with copies of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within five working days from execution in order to give time for the review, evaluation and recommendations to management, if needed.
observations.reason: Only contracts for infrastructure projects together with the corresponding supporting documents were submitted to the Team. Management committed to submit purchase orders and their supporting documents.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to adjust the accounts as presented above and thereafter instruct him to account transactions accordingly based on their actual nature using applicable accounting laws, rules, and regulations;

Require the Municipal Engineer to provide cost of the demolished parts/structure to the Municipal Accountant so that the latter will be able to derecognize the said parts/structures in the book of accounts; and

Instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant on the classification of programs, projects, and activities during budget preparation to ensure that financial reports will reflect the actual intent of Management for these programs.

     We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct:

The Municipal Engineer to furnish copies of GPPA Report to the Municipal Accountant that so that completed projects are assured to be included in the financial statements. 

The Municipal Accountant to recognize Flood Control Systems account amounting to ₱564,462.86 to recognize completed project as at December 31, 2020.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately adjust the affected accounts by recognizing Road Networks and the associated Accumulated Depreciation amounting to ₱25,830,825.91 and ₱3,890,722.03, respectively. Moreover, restate the affected accounts in the comparative financial statements of the incoming year as well as provide additional disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements to support the required restatement.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant checked and adjusted accordingly all affected accounts.
observations.reason: The said accounts were adjusted as can be seen in Note 15 of the Notes to the FS.

observations.recommendation:
Municipal Budget Officer to maintain Registries of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations, so he could religiously prepare monthly Status of Appropriation, Allotment, and Obligation and furnish the Municipal Accountant in a timely manner. 



Municipal Accountant to reconcile his records with the SAAOB of the Municipal Budget Officer before finalizing the financial statements to ensure that all transactions are completely recognized on the appropriate budget year.  


Municipal Treasurer to immediately transmit monthly Disbursement Vouchers and supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant to facilitate timely recording of financial transactions.
observations.managementAction:
Municipal Budget Officer maintains Registries of Appropriation and Obligations and prepare and furnishes monthly SAAO to the Municipal Accountant, to facilitate faster recording of transactions and to have enough time to reconcile his records from other reports.

Municipal Accountant with the assistance of the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer developed a system that would produce a smooth processing of the information tailored with the current available manpower and facilities of the Municipality.

Continue training staffs in the finance offices to help in the processing and recording of transactions.
observations.reason: The Budget Officer already maintains RAO and SAAO to be reconciled by the Accounting Office on its books of accounts.

observations.recommendation:
Require the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer to judiciously review and authorize transactions based on budgeted amounts to avoid technical issues that would arise from excessive disbursement of funds as a result otherwise.

Enjoin responsible officers to prepare resolutions for augmentation of savings or appropriation ordinance for supplemental budgets in case additional funds will be needed for programs with lacking budgeted amount as deemed necessary.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Treasurer judiciously monitors transactions based on the authorized budgets and to initiate actions needed, such as request for augmentation of savings in case a transaction deemed necessary could not be supported with the authorized budget.

Technical issues, such as securing right of way and erroneous labeling of programs or projects, be eliminated to ensure implementation of planned projects or program and realization of target benefits therefrom.

Municipal Budget Officer to follow-up funding requests with partner agencies before finalizing the Annual Investment Plan to make sure that funds will be efficiently distributed.
observations.reason: Said project was realigned to other developmental project.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to update the Gender and Development Database since data gathering during distribution of relief goods and the DSWD-SAP has already been made. 

Encourage responsible offices or departments to utilize the said database in developing their programs, projects, and activities to address social issues, such as the negative effects of the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.managementAction: MSWDO as the GAD Focal Person immediately updated GAD Database and used the updated database in strategizing programs, projects, and activities to address social issues, such as the negative effects of the Covid-19 pandemic.
observations.reason: GAD Database of the Municipality were updated and used in strategizing programs, projects, and activities to address social issues, such as the negative effects of the Covid-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to locate and submit immediately the liquidation documents of the unliquidated cash advances; refrain from transferring his cash advances to another; and thereafter, liquidate within the prescribed period and submit immediately the liquidation documents to the Municipal Accountant.

Avoid paying thru cash; adopt public bidding as a general mode of procurement; refrain from granting cash advance to a single employee or official in behalf of a group of employees/officials; and require those who are granted cash advances to immediately liquidate within the prescribed period to avoid withholding of their salary and/or other sanctions.
observations.managementAction:
Management to submit liquidation documents for the remaining unliquidated cash advances of previous year.





Cash advances were granted to directly to officers and employees.
observations.reason: Registration is already paid directly to organizer/host groups and transportation is directly paid to transportation group/company. Per diem is the only CA allowed for personnel and strongly discouraged to only one payee.

observations.recommendation: Expedite action on the findings and recommendations of the Office of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan in order to adjust/update municipalities’ taxes and rate pursuant to Section 191 of Republic Act 7160 and eventually, increased its local income to finance various programs/projects/activities that are beneficial to its constituents. Thereafter, monitor the passage of ordinances to ensure that these are approved and implemented without delay.
observations.managementAction: Management to effect amendments noted in the Tax Ordinance as reviewed by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.
observations.reason: The Municipality used the new tax ordinance in their collection of taxes during CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately locate the missing Cash Tickets and require her to submit a written explanation on how the shortage occurred to evade the issuance of a Notice of Charge by the Auditor against the accountable officer as provided by Sections 101(1) and 102(1) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The cash tickets were already located and included in the inventory of accountable forms.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer be required to maintain cashbooks by bank account and by fund to properly monitor the deposits of collections and withdrawals of cash and the balance of her accountabilities as well.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasury Office (MTO) already maintained a separate Cashbook for LBP Account Number 0062-1005-24 Account for Online Payment) and all transactions were already recorded therein.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer ascertain that all the collecting officers have complied with the requirement of Section 101 of PD 1445 prior to handling the collection of local taxes or fees. Thus, requiring Ms. Roselyn M. Legaspi to secure Fidelity Bond from the Fidelity Bond Division of the Bureau of the Treasury.
observations.managementAction: The Fidelity Bond for Ms. Roselyn M. Legaspi, Municipal Civil Registrar, has been secured from the Bureau of Treasury.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and cause the submission of the required Reports of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) in its prescribed form to the Office of the Auditor and strictly comply with the requirements provided by the afore-cited regulation in order to facilitate the inventory of the accountable forms; and the Treasurer should diligently monitor the appropriateness and correctness of the documents/reports being submitted to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasury Office MTO) already submitted the revised Reports of Accountability for Accountable Forms (RAAF) in its prescribed format.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management: 

Conduct regular reconciliation of records and reports to be undertaken by the Treasury and Accounting Offices at the end of each month or at least quarterly prior to the preparation of financial reports to avoid accumulation of unreconciled variances; and

Effect the necessary adjusting entries in their respective records to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The management is already undertaking a regular monthly reconciliation of records between Treasury and Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management provide an explanation on the causes of decrease in the value of land account and provide the Audit Team with the necessary data or information for reference in audit.
observations.managementAction: The management already submitted the details to support the decrease in the Account Title “Land”. This pertains to the Sale of Lot at Centro 2, Luna Isabela which was previously acquired at Goldstar enterprises and held for sale to the settlers and to the public.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accounting Office and the OIC-General Services Office to verify and analyze the causes of discrepancies in the PPE accounts and make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts and in the RPCPPE.
observations.managementAction: The management initially determined that the discrepancy pertains to the various Public Infrastructure projects completed over the past years were not taken up in the records of the designated General Services Officer (GSO). The management is yet to conduct a Physical Inventory Count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to investigate the recorded and unrecorded; if there’s any, PPE items in the books of accounts.  Obtain and keep sufficient proofs of their existence and maintain subsidiary ledger for each of these PPE items that shall reconcile with and support the general ledger balances.
observations.managementAction: The management recovered some files for the result of the most recent Inventory Count conducted last CY 2015. However, due to incomplete details, SL were not completely prepared.
The management thru the Municipal Engineering office prepared an initial list of all the Road Networks within the Municipality.
List of COVID Patients was submitted.

The grant of AHDP was based on the Administrative Order No. 26 series of 2020 issued by the Office of the President last March 23, 2020.

Liquidation Report was submitted without supporting documents.

The Management commits to submit other required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly adhere with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 and JMC No. 1 dated March 27, 2020 in the preparation of Annual Investment Plan to ensure that all items to be funded are development programs/projects and activities that partakes the nature of investment or capital expenditures.
observations.managementAction: The management already submitted the necessary work programs and statement of work accomplished to substantiate the expenditures in the implementation of various projects and wages of laborers assigned in the River Control Management Project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the persons liable to immediately settle their accounts including settlement of the old account.
observations.managementAction: The management made a demand to the liable individual but to no avail.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management continuously remit to the concerned government agencies the respective amounts withheld/collected within the prescriptive period of remittance.
observations.managementAction: The management ensured that contributions withheld for PHIC, HDMF and GSIS were remitted properly within the prescribed period.

The management already made an adjustment to the “Investment Property – Land” Account to its appropriate account.
However, the other recommendations are yet to be implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management: 

a. ensure that all officials/employees settle their accounts within the time set by pertinent rules and regulations; and

b. if the probability of collection for the amount of ₱368,061.40 is remote, file request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts to COA Office, duly supported with documents mentioned under paragraph 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.  Otherwise, absent of said documents enumerated in this Circular, management may file appropriate charge against this former accountable officer, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The management ensured that all cash advances granted were liquidated within the prescribed period, except that of Mr. Carlos G. Lampitoc which was granted under the previous administration. Request for Write off was not made due to the incompleteness of documents as required under COA Circular No. 2016-005.
The management exerted efforts in retrieving available records/data for these various Asset and Liability accounts but inquiries and data gathered were not enough to substantiate these accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendations that the Municipal Accountant be required to:

a. Prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and

b. Perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions.
observations.managementAction: The management exerted efforts in retrieving available records/data for the reconciliation of these accounts but to no avail since no data was turned-over by the previous accountant to support these accounts.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that management should make representation with the DA-RFU-02 for the return of the equipment as embodied in the loan agreement.   Coordinate with the said agency for the issuance of clearance as basis in dropping loans payable account in the books.
observations.managementAction: The management already made representations with DA-RFO2 to return the equipment as embodied in the signed Agreement but the agency refused to accept such.

observations.recommendation: With these afore-cited observations, we recommended that management comply with the provision of Budget Circular No. 2007-3 and Budget Circular No. 2004-5A on the payment of honoraria.  We further recommended that the BAC members, TWG and BAC Secretariat be required to refund the excess amount received.   Moreover, the applicable tax be applied for the said honoraria.
observations.managementAction: The necessary documents to support the honoraria to BAC, BAC Secretariat and BAC TWG were already submitted. The excess honoraria on the other hand were already returned.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management to:

a. Submit Monthly Report of Official Travels for every motor vehicle, duly prepared by the Driver concerned summarizing in chronological order his trips for the month; and

b. Submit Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption for every government motor transportation containing among others, the different types of motor vehicles utilized by the LGU during the month, total distance traveled, total fuel used and the normal fuel consumption for each vehicle.

c. We further recommended management to ensure that the trip tickets are properly accomplished and duly approved by authorized officials before embarking on a trip.
observations.managementAction: The management regularly prepares the required documentation and attached said report in the payment of Fuel and Oils to substantiate such expenses. Also, Driver’s Trip ticket were properly accomplished.

The management ensured that accountability has been established and the implementation of the project has been closely monitored by those in charge of the program.

The Management commits to submit the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit/furnish copies of contracts and Purchase Orders within five (5) days upon approval and Notices of Delivery and Inspection and Acceptance Reports within twenty-four (24) hours from receipt of delivery to the Audit Team for review. Strictly observe the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-001 and COA Circular No. 96-010 on the submission of all future contracts and its supporting documents including copies of notices of deliveries, inspection and acceptance within the stated period.
observations.managementAction: The management already submitted the necessary documents for these projects and Technical Inspection were already conducted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the person liable for the disallowed amount of ₽5,000.00 to immediately settle the account including settlement of the old account.
observations.managementAction: With similar observation reiterated in CY 2020 audit.

Not Implemented
The MPDC admitted that GAD database is not yet established. However, a province-wide update on Community-Based Monitoring System (CBMS) which will be one of the bases of the formulation of the GAD database will be conducted by the PGA.
observations.reason: The MPDC admitted that GAD database is not yet established. However, a province-wide update on Community-Based Monitoring System (CBMS) which will be one of the bases of the formulation of the GAD database will be conducted by the PGA.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the 

The General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the required Property Cards and renew the AREs in order to update his records;
The Inventory Committee to conduct inventory and use the updated records of the GSO and Municipal Accountant to facilitate conduct of inventory and preparation of report; and
The Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to regularly reconcile PPE records with the RPCPPE as required in Section 57 of MNGAS to ensure that records maintained are complete and accurate and assets recorded are actually existing.
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to recognize in the books the unrecorded road networks with their proportionate Accumulated Depreciation. Moreover, review reports and disbursement vouchers from previous years for similar transactions incurred in Due to NGAs account and appropriately account them in the book of accounts to ensure that all local road transactions are fairly presented in the Financial Statements.
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:

Municipal Accountant to trace and duly account local road network transactions prior to CY 2016 through journals, disbursement vouchers, supporting documents and other reports. Thereafter, provide the necessary data to the Municipal Engineer and GSO in the conduct of physical inventory of local roads;
Further, for him to include in the Financial Statements the necessary disclosures related to Local Road Networks (Annex B) as well as prepare subsidiary ledgers for the different road components to be reconciled with the property cards of the GSO-Designate as prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015.
GSO-Designate with the Inventory Committee to finalize the inventory of local roads including the inventory of road components using the prescribed formats, Annexes A and C of COA Circular No. 2015-008.
Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and GSO- Designate the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.managementAction:
General Services Officer did not prepare and maintain Property Cards of the different PPEs
Inventory Committee conducted inventory of PPEs maintained by the different department/offices of the Municipality.
Inventory Committee conducted inventory of PPEs maintained by the different department/offices of the Municipality.
The Municipal Accountant did not prepare adjustment in the book of accounts due to voluminous work and prioritization of processing and recording of COVID-19 related transaction. Further, review of prior year’s records of the trust fund was not made, as well.
Municipal Accountant did not revisit previous records for local road networks prior to CY 2016.
Municipal Accountant also did not disclose the required information per COA Circular No. 2015-008 dated November 25, 2015. On the other hand, subsidiary ledgers were prepared only for those recorded from CY 2018 to date.
Management prioritized provision of relief to affected families of the COVID-19 pandemic.
Management prioritized provision of relief to affected families of the COVID-19 pandemic.
observations.reason:
Inventory report only included various equipment of the different departments/offices of the Municipality.
Only those inventoried during the year was reconciled with the PPE records.
Ledgers of the account did not contain required adjustments of the account.
Ledgers for local road networks were only limited to completed and recorded projects from CY 2018 to date.
Inventory report did not include local road networks of the Municipality.
Required information was not prepared.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct:

The Municipal Treasurer to record those presented in Annex B to have a more reliable cashbook. Likewise, ensure that all transactions pertaining to cash are recorded timely in the cashbook and all checks issued are recorded in the Report of Checks Issued and submitted to the Municipal Accountant;
The Municpal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in timely recording the transactions of the Municipality.
Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to reconcile their records and bank statements regularly and make the necessary adjustments based on reconciliation made.
observations.managementAction:
Municipal Treasurer made adjustments in the cashbook pertaining to CY 2019 errors made. Moreover, transactions were recorded in the cashbook on a monthly basis.
Municipal Accountant issued demand letters to the Municipal Treasurer to expedite submission of documents and recording of transactions.
Municipal Accountant reconciled records with the Cashbooks during preparation of the Annual Financial Statements of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
Inquiry during cash examination revealed that monthly recording in the Cashbook was not sustained. Unreleased checks were still recorded in the Cashbook and no Report of Checks Issued was prepared.
Interim Financial Statements were not made while submission of the Annual Financial Statements was delayed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the discrepancies noted and remit to the concerned agencies the balances of amount withheld to avoid penalties and surcharges. Likewise, we recommended that Management remit their contributions within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: Management identified the variances on a monthly basis.
observations.reason: Discrepancies remained unadjusted in the books as Management were still validating for additional remittance to the said agencies or refund to affected employees.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the Municipal Budget Officer to immediately obligate programs/projects in full amount of contract cost upon completion of the said projects. Thereafter, for the Municipal Accountant to duly recognize Guarantee/Security Deposits Payable to reflect the actual and true nature of the transactions pertaining to infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Budget Officer obligated transactions with the same nature in full amount of the contract cost. On the other hand, the Municipal Accountant recorded such transactions as PPE in full amount of contract cost while recognizing Guarantee/Security Deposits Payable for withheld retention money.
observations.reason: Paid infrastructure projects were duly recorded in full amount of the contract cost with corresponding Guarantee/Security Deposits Payable for withheld retention money. However, completed infrastructure project as of year-end amounting ₱564,462.86 was not recorded in the books. Discussed in part II of this report.

observations.managementAction: Management hired three (3) Job Orders to complement personnel vacancies in Treasury Office.
observations.reason:
Collections still used receipts to replenish petty cash fund for petty operating expenses and AICS. Details are discussed in part II.
Collections were not deposited intact as Management still used receipts to replenish petty cash fund. Moreover, there was still significant delay in the deposit of collections. Discussed in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing observations, we have recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor all cash advances, including those of the disbursing officer; make the necessary adjustments to reflect the true balance of the Advances accounts; and thereafter, record only the amount liquidated and immediately verify the liquidation documents upon submission and prior to granting of another cash advance.
Moreover, direct the Municipal Treasurer to locate and submit immediately the liquidation documents of the unliquidated cash advances; refrain from transferring his cash advances to another; and thereafter, liquidate within the prescribed period and submit immediately the liquidation documents to the Municipal Accountant.
Likewise, we have recommended that Management:

Avoid paying thru cash; adopt public bidding as a general mode of procurement; refrain from granting cash advance to a single employee or official in behalf of a group of employees/officials; and require those who are granted cash advances to immediately liquidate within the prescribed period to avoid withholding of their salary and/or other sanctions.
observations.managementAction:
Management included in the monitoring and reporting of cash advances regular advances of funds. Moreover, special cash advances were limited to transportation, accommodation and meals.
Management submitted liquidation documents for the remaining unliquidated cash advances of previous year.
Cash advances were granted directly to officers or employees with official business.
observations.reason:
Moreover, special cash advances were limited to transportation, accommodation and meals.
Further inquiry with Municipal Treasurer revealed that cash advances for Special Amelioration Program of the DSWD and financial assistance to affected constituents of ASF was transferred to other personnel to expedite distribution of the said funds.
While checks for cash advances were issued to officers or employees with official business, meals and snacks that could have been aggregated and procured as a group was not made. Further, payment that should have been paid through checks such as remittance of Documentary Stamp Taxes to BIR was still paid through the Petty Cash Fund. Lastly, all cash advances were liquidated as of year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Head of the Procuring Entity:
 direct the Bids and Awards Committee and Technical Working Group to conduct post qualification by verifying, validating, and ascertaining all statements made and documents submitted by the bidder with the Lowest Calculated Bid and ensure that the Post-Qualification Report contains in-depth results on the verification of technical requirements.
instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and Technical Working Group, in cases there would be simultaneous or overlapping projects to be awarded to the same contractor, to require the contractor to augment its key personnel and equipment to be committed to the project in order to properly determine the responsiveness of the LCB to the eligibility and bid requirements.  Otherwise, disqualify future bids whose key personnel and equipment are already pledged / used in other on-going projects. Also, direct the Municipal Engineer to monitor simultaneous implementation of projects by the same contractor to avoid unnecessary delays or substandard quality of work.
observations.managementAction:
The BAC and TWG validated technical documents submitted by the bidders through visitation to their offices as well as evaluated bidders capability in performing required program of work through the availability of equipment and manpower of the bidders.
Management monitored implementation of projects simultaneously and ensured that projects were completed within schedule.
observations.reason:
Auditorial Contract Review showed that post qualification was only made in one (1) day and Management failed to require justifications from winning bidders negative slippage noted in their attached Statement of All On-Going Contracts. Discussed in part II of this report.
Review of Government Report on Programs, Projects, and Activities showed that projects started were completed as scheduled.

observations.recommendation:
In view of the foregoing, we have recommended the management to:

Require the Implementing/End-User Units to prepare the PPMPs in accordance with Section 7 of RA 9184 using the suggested format by the GPPB duly posted in its website and include all infrastructure projects in the preparation of PPMP;
Require the Budget Office to thoroughly review and evaluate the submitted PPMPs in terms of amount and its coherence with the proposed budget of the LGU; and
Require the BAC through the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the PPMPs and prepare the APP in accordance to the applicable provisions of RA 9184 using the prescribed format under GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 and GPPB Resolution No. 20-2015 dated July 16, 2015.
observations.managementAction:
The different departments/offices prepared their PPMPs in accordance with Section 7 of RA 9184 using the suggested format by the GPPB.
The Municipal Budget Officer reviewed and evaluated the PPMPs in terms of amount and its coherence with the proposed budget.
Management prepared the APP in accordance with the suggested format.
observations.reason:
While PPMPs of the different department were prepared with regard to the Annual Budget, supplemental PPMPs were not prepared for reprogrammed projects, programs, and activities.
There was no PPMPs prepared for supplemental budgets.
GPPB suggested format for APP was not followed which should have included schedule and mode of procurement. Instead, management only listed various items to be procured in the 1st, 2nd, 3rd, and 4th Quarter of CY 2020. Further, APP was not revised to include procurement of PPAs in the supplemental budgets. Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
Based from the foregoing, we have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to:

Automatically release the Special Education Tax Income to the SEF and timely recognize in the cashbooks the collections and deposits; and
Prepare and submit Abstract of Collection for Real Property Tax to the Municipal Accountant regularly to facilitate review of distribution of real property tax collection.
observations.managementAction:
Management transferred SET income to the Special Education Fund.
Management prepared Abstract of Collection for Real Property Tax.
observations.reason:
Review of records showed that transfer of SET income from the local treasury was only transferred on a monthly basis instead of date of remittance from liquidating officer to the Municipal Treasurer.
Presentation of the report showed detailed Abstract of Collection for Real Property Taxes as required.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management expedite its action on the findings and recommendations of the Office of the Sangguniang Panlalawigan in order to adjust/update the municipality’s taxes and rates pursuant to Section 191 of Republic Act 7160 and eventually, increase its local income to finance various programs/projects/activities that are beneficial to its constituents. Thereafter, monitor the passage of ordinances to ensure that these are approved and implemented without delay.
observations.managementAction: Management had already effected amendments noted in the Tax Ordinance as reviewed by the Sangguniang Panlalawigan.
observations.reason: The Municipality used the old tax ordinance for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management require:

Additional payment for the uncollected penalties during the CY 2019. Thereafter, strictly implement with the penalty provisions of the current Tax Ordinance, requiring assessment and collection of penalties for delayed payments of business taxes as additional income or funds for projects to be implemented as well as ensure earlier collection of the said funds; and
The certified copy of Annual Income Tax Return to Business Taxpayers on the second quarter of the year and adjust the business tax and collect the difference if there are discrepancies between the amount appearing in the ITR and gross sales declared in the business tax application form.
observations.managementAction:
Management prepared demand letters for the uncollected penalties in CY 2019. On the other hand, the Treasury Office made clarification with the Legislative Office for the application of appropriate penalty rate of interest.
Management failed to require submission of certified copy of Annual Income Tax Returns from Business Taxpayers in contrast with their existing tax ordinance.
observations.reason: Review of records showed that inadequate penalties from CY 2019 remained uncollected as of year-end. Moreover, while penalties were strictly imposed, the Municipal Treasury still underapplied penalties in contrast with our recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management strategically schedule GAD projects and vigilantly monitor its implementation so that its purpose will be actually realized and enjoyed by majority of the community during the year.
Moreover, we recommended that Management update their GAD Database with latest necessary data required in Annex B of JMC 2013-001 to have a more reliable basis in identifying priority gender issues to be addressed for the coming year. Thereafter, judiciously conduct gender analysis using the updated GAD Database in identifying priority gender issues and formulating high-impact programs/projects/activities to address such during planning and budgeting of GAD Funds.
observations.managementAction:
Management had implemented programs and activities under GAD as scheduled.
Management failed to update the GAD Database in CY 2020.
observations.reason:
Examination of the GAD accomplishment report showed that only 8 out of the 18 of the programs and activities under GAD was implemented during the year. Further, only 65.94% of the fund was utilized.
Current GAD focal person disclosed that she has yet to review the GAD Database while former GAD focal person further disclosed that the database was updated three years ago.
