We recommended that Management, thru the Municipal Accounting Office, to reconcile the discrepancy between the two ledgers. If the unreconciled balance is carried over from previous years, maintain this balance then work backwards.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to monitor closely the prompt transmittal of reports to the accounting office to facilitate the prompt preparation of remittances to the other LGUs.
observations.managementAction: Only the shares for the 4th Quarter 2018 were not yet remitted as of year-end.
observations.reason: The transmittal of reports from the Treasury Office to the Accounting Office was still late; hence, the delayed remittances to other LGUs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management to submit the disbursement vouchers completely supported with the required documents.
observations.reason: Contracts and supporting documents, though incomplete, were submitted to the Audit Team. Lacking supporting documents are communicated to the Management after the conduct of auditorial review of the contracts. .

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the performance security before the entering into a contract agreement with the winning bidder.
We recommended that Management adhere to the requisite provisions of RA 9184 on the postponement of bid opening.

We recommended that Management:

fast track the implementation of priority programs and projects for the benefits of its citizens. In the event that there was physical but no financial accomplishment, this should be disclosed in the accomplishment report of Management for validation. 



evaluate projects funded under MDF 2010-2014  if still implementable, otherwise, revert funds for re-appropriation.
observations.managementAction: Management prioritized the implementation of infrastructure projects funded by national agencies. Moreover, unfavorable weather conditions had caused the delay in the implementation of various projects.

Partially Implemented

Some prior years’ projects have been re- aligned into another project.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to deduct the creditable income tax and final VAT from payments to suppliers as provided under RMO No. 23-2014.
observations.managementAction: According to the Municipal Accountant, they had already discussed with the supplier on the imposition of VAT and CIT. However, the supplier stated that it will be added to the selling price which will be an additional cost to be incurred by the Municipality.
observations.reason: In CY 2018, VAT and CIT were not yet deducted from the payment to the supplier.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

monitor cash advances to avoid accumulation; and
notify the concerned officials and employees of their accountabilities and demand for the immediate settlement thereof, including imposing sanctions or withholding of salaries in case of non-compliance, pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 14.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor stated during the exit conference that cash advances for travel must be liquidated within 30 days from arrival to the official station. Otherwise, it will be deducted from the salary of the employee who fails to liquidate the same.
observations.reason:
As of December 31, 2018, the unliquidated cash advances decreased to ₱2,607,078.92


Fully Implemented

Concerned officials and employees have been notified of their accountabilities and Management requested for the immediate settlement thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management through the BAC stop procuring goods out-rightly through direct contracting and emergency purchase, rather, consider widening its pool of suppliers to obtain the best deal for the government as required under the Procurement Act.
observations.managementAction: As discussed in the exit conference, these suppliers were the only ones who are willing to sell supplies and materials thru credit.
observations.reason: For CY 2018, some supplies and materials were already purchased thru public bidding. However, some were still procured out-rightly thru direct contracting and emergency purchase.

observations.managementAction: Management commits to look into the matter and collect/impose the said amount from the contractor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Social Worker and the concerned Day Care Worker prepare daily menu, and monitoring and growth chart of the beneficiaries as a tool for evaluating the effectiveness of the program. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor assign a person-in-charge to monitor the Supplemental Feeding.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze the composition of the balance of the Accounts Payable and those without binding claims have to be reverted to the net asset/equity of the LGU.
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer restitute immediately the full amount of his cash shortage.

We have recommended that the accountant:

prepare a journal entry voucher to record the actual cash transfer of the LDRRMF amounting to ₱1,336,476.58 to the trust liability account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002
observations.reason: The balance of the LDRRMF has been transferred to the account “Trust Liability – DRRM.” However, it is not transferred in the Special Trust Fund but maintained in the General Fund.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) adopt necessary measures to ensure compliance by the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to existing laws and regulations for the timely submission of accounts or reports of the LGU’s transactions, otherwise, sanctions maybe instituted, if warranted.
observations.managementAction:
According to the Municipal Accountant, the delay in the transmittal of disbursement vouchers is due to delayed/un-claimed checks by the payees.

The Acting Municipal Treasurer committed to promptly submit RCD and ORs to the Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Disbursement Vouchers for December 2018 were only submitted on April 2, 2019. RCD and ORs were only submitted during the conduct of cash examination.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management furnish the COA auditor with copies of contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within five working days from execution in order to give time for the review, evaluation and recommendations to management, if needed.
observations.managementAction:
According to the MPDC, the BAC does not have a separate office or work area, hence, files were stored in different offices. This might have caused the delay in the transmittal of contracts and its supporting documents.

The Municipal Mayor committed to provide a work area for the BAC.
observations.reason: Contracts together with the corresponding supporting documents have been submitted to the Team albeit not promptly.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Chief Executive through the Municipal Fire Marshall and GSO need to follow-up the delivery of the fire truck. Know the reasons of the delay from the Federation and employ measures so that a refund of the amount paid be done if they could no longer make delivery of the fire truck.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to settle the balance of ₱100,000.00 until August 31, 2019.
observations.reason: A COA Order of Execution has been issued and settlements have been made, leaving a balance of ₱100,000.00.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting entry to restore the total amount of ₱2,192,147.19 to the Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account and corresponding payable accounts.  Also, the Municipal Treasurer to notify the payees of stale checks so that new check will be issued before these checks will become outstanding for two years.

Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant list of unreleased checks as at year-end.  Further, the Municipal Accountant to prepare JEV to restore to Cash in Bank – Local Currency, Current Account and corresponding payable accounts.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entry has been recorded and the Accounting Department already provided the list of checks to the Acting Municipal Treasurer for notification action. The recommendation is partially implemented due to the voluminous transactions (backlogs) that the available personnel’s capacity is not enough to accommodate.

observations.recommendation: 2.1. We recommended that the LCE to enjoin the Inventory Committee to include in the conduct of inventory of the properties of the municipality the infrastructures and other community facilities.  We further recommended that the Inventory Committee prepare and submit the required Report to the DILG and for the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Registry of Public Infrastructure and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: The management has already searched the official Registry of Public Infrastructure form. Further, the Municipal Accountant has set a meeting with GSO Designate and Mun Eng’r on how this can be implemented and agreed to  conduct annual inventory on December 2018.
observations.reason: Per COA validation, no physical count of Public Infrastructures has been conducted.

observations.recommendation: 3.1. We have recommended that the Local Finance Committee, prepare the Annual Investment Plan for 20% Development Fund based on the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 to avoid confusion among the readers / users.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee was already informed for its compliance.

observations.recommendation:
4.1. The Municipal Accountant to 

i.	Prepare subsidiary ledgers for LDRRMF by year in order to determine the balance that could be reverted if remain unutilized for the preceding five years.

ii.	Adjust the amounts accordingly in the Financial Statements and Report on the Receipts and Utilization for DRRM.

	Further, the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records.

	Lastly, to revert unutilized 2011 and 2012 appropriations for LDRRM to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has already reconciled previous (2017) balances and has already requested the Acting Municipal Treasurer for the transfer of adjusted and unexpended previous year fund to Special Trust Fund.
observations.reason: The recommendation has been fully complied with per validation.

observations.recommendation: 5.1. We recommended the Local Chief Executive to ensure strict observance of the pertinent provisions of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 and to instruct concerned officials to prepare the Annual Procurement Plan (APP) as provided in the aforesaid provisions, which shall be the basis for procurement by the Municipality for proper planning of all procurement activities to attain efficiency, economy and effectiveness in its procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation has been fully complied with for the 2019 budget.

observations.recommendation: 5.2. We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation for the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the requirements as provided in the above cited provisions of laws, rules and regulations particularly on the procurement of medicines.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation has been fully complied with.

observations.recommendation: 5.3. We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC Chairman to require future contractors to submit Construction Safety and Health Program approved by the DOLE as attachment to the contract.
observations.managementAction: The BAC discussed on the requirement needed and agreed to make it available moving forward. Further, a policy was established that no/incomplete document requirements, no payment.

observations.recommendation: 5.4. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the person responsible to properly fill up information in the Purchase Request (PR), Purchase Order (PO), Request for Quotation (RFQ) and Inspection and Acceptance Report (IAR) to easily determine reasonableness of the transaction and compliance with the requirements of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: The management required log book to monitor and log numbering of PR, PO and IAR.

observations.recommendation: 5.5. We recommended that management comply, particularly the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC), regularly procure common-use supplies, materials and equipment which are necessary in its operations from the Procurement Service – Department of Budget and Management as provided under the above-cited provision to contribute to the attainment of a modernized and standardized government procurement system and to avail of the most economic price in the acquisition of supplies and materials.
observations.managementAction: The Agency already procures its common-use supplies from the Procurement Service.

observations.recommendation: 5.6. We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee agreed to follow the provisions of Sections 37.1.6 and 37.4.2 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in the posting of Notice of Award, approved contract and Notice to Proceed to avoid delay of publishing such aforesaid documents in PhilGEPS and to ensure transparency in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: The internet response has been improved thereby resulting to prompt posting of Notice of Award, contracts, and Notice to Proceed in Philgeps.

observations.recommendation:
5.8. We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Inspectorate Team to conduct inspection on completed projects after the one (1) year defects liability period and issue the corresponding Certificate of Final Acceptance to the contractors if no defects and deficiencies were detected. Nevertheless, if construction defects and failures were detected upon inspection, then corresponding measures must be undertaken by the contractor to correct them.

	Further, to oblige the contractor to post warranty security that will answer structural defects/failures that may be discovered from final acceptance of the project up to the period prescribed in Section 62.2.3.2 to ensure that the contractor will perform his responsibilities as prescribed in Section 62.2.3.1 (a) of the revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

	Also, for the management to furnish the audit team copies of Certificate of Final Acceptance for completed projects to monitor the posting of the said warranty security by the contractors.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Engineer already discussed with Inspectorate Team and contractors on the inspection and issuance of certificate of final acceptance.

observations.recommendation: 6.1. We commend management in immediately acting positively on our audit recommendation, we therefore recommended for management to update themselves on issuances (Circulars, Memoranda, Executive Orders and Administrative Orders) of National Government Agencies and laws that would affect their delivery of basic services and transactions.
observations.managementAction: Conduct of extension classes thru Local School Board Teachers had already stopped its operation.

observations.recommendation:
8.1. We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation and the management guaranteed the audit team to develop enforcement technique such as ordinances for effective and stricter implementation of the provisions of RA No. 9003.

	Further, though there is a big gap between knowledge and practice, we recommend that the Municipality intensify dissemination and enhancing awareness of constituents on RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction:
From 14/40 or 35% in 2017 MRF compliance of Brgys, additional 14 complied as of August 2018. It was noted also from Barangay Treasurer’s that 6 is on process.
Projected 2018 compliance is 85%.

observations.recommendation: 9.1. We recommended that the municipality thru the Municipal Social Welfare and Development (MSWD) Office adhere to the provisions of DSWD Department Order No. 22 in order to ensure the proper implementation of the program and ascertain the validity and propriety of the transaction.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation is duly complied with.

The management has already recovered the said amount and appropriate Journal Entries has been made.
The management prepares the Monthly Bank Reconciliation and submits the same to the COA Office.
Except for the overdue accounts of two accountable officers, management ensured that all cash advances granted for the year has been liquidated within the prescribed period.

The management exerted efforts to look into the details of the dormant receivable accounts by inquiring to the former Municipal Accountant and searching available records, yet to no avail, cannot properly support the said accounts, hence the management would like to request that such accounts should be written-off.
The management exerted efforts to review the said accounts but no available records on file, hence cannot validate its correctness and should be requested for write-off.

The management exerted efforts to reconcile the said accounts but failed to, due to the unavailability of records.  However, financial records as at December 31, 2018 balance was decreased to ₱255,524.29.
The management is submitting its accounts and financial reports to COA on a timely basis.

The management strictly complied with the provisions set forth on the guidelines on the utilization of the 20% Economic Development Fund (EDF) except for some transactions which were discussed under Part II, Finding No. 18.
The management already maintains a separate bank account for the LDRRMF which was opened last June 2014. Also the management submits to COA office its monthly report on the utilization of LDDRMF.

The management ensured that the appropriation for Senior Citizen, PWDs and for the Implementations of Programs of the Local Council for the Protection of Children is sufficient enough by not merely a single line item in the budget but also by attribution in the different programs of the Municipality.

The management provided the necessary reports and submitted the same to the office of the Auditor. Also, the required posting of information and publicity on programs/ projects/ activities has been complied with by the management thru the office of the Municipal Engineer.
The management submitted the required documents to the COA office for review of the contracts and inspection of deliveries.
Properties of the LGU were already insured with the GSIS.

The office of the GSO Designate facilitated the crafting of design for the markings of all the Municipal Vehicles and properly installed such markings in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 75-6.
As of December 31, 2018, the balance was reduced to ₱418,097.94.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Accounting Unit maintain complete property ledger cards instead of subsidiary ledgers with details or breakdown.

We reiterate our prior year’s audit recommendation for the LCE require the Supply Officer Designate to submit RPCPPE. Further, for the GSO - Designate and OIC - Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records.

We have recommended that the Local Finance Committee, prepare the Annual Investment Plan for 20% Development Fund based on the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-01 dated February 22, 2017 to avoid confusion among the readers / users.

We recommended for the Local Chief Executive to direct the:
Municipal Budget Officer to:

Determine the correct balances of the Current Appropriations, Continuing Appropriations and Trust Liabilities.
The Municipal Accountant to

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation for the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to comply with the requirements as provided in the above cited provisions of laws, rules and regulations particularly on the procurement of medicines.
We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC Chairman to require future contractors to submit Construction Safety and Health Program approved by the DOLE as attachment to the contract.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee ensure that the posted Performance Bond completely bear all the essential information especially the date of issuance prior to issuance of the Notice to Proceed.

No. 9184.
Also, for the management to furnish the audit team copies of Certificate of Final Acceptance for completed projects to monitor the posting of the said warranty security by the contractors.

We commend management in immediately acting positively on our audit recommendation, we therefore recommended for management to update themselves on issuances (Circulars, Memoranda, Executive Orders and Administrative Orders) of National Government Agencies and laws that would affect their delivery of basic services and transactions.

We `recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to submit on time the disbursement vouchers, payrolls and official receipts to the Auditor within ten (10) days from date of receipt of said documents.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation for the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to submit the required interim financial statements together with the Status of Appropriations, Allotments, and Obligations in accordance with Section 70 and 73 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I to enable the audit team to review and analyze the financial statements every quarter.

We recommended that the municipality continue to appropriate at least 5% of their total annual budget for gender and development plans and programs to ensure the promotion of public welfare and basic services addressing gender issues.
We recommended that management continue to identify programs and projects that address gender issues and promote gender equality and women empowerment.

We recommend that the concerned municipal officials and employees fully implement programs/projects/activities in accordance with DILG-approved Gad Plan and Budget to fully address gender issues and women empowerment of the constituents. Further, for the GFPS to ensure and monitor strict implementation of the annual GAD PPAs for the constituents to fully utilize the benefit that could be derived therefrom.
We recommend that the GAD Focal person submit the Annual GAD Plan and Budget and corresponding Accomplishment Report within the prescribed period to facilitate timely review of the same.

We recommended and management positively agreed that aside from the initial water tests, they will see to it that periodic examination of water will be made.  Further, to ensure protection against contamination of water they will ask the Sangguniang Bayan through the initiative of the Municipal Health Officer to pass an ordinance on the Protection of Water Sources against contamination.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation for the WATSAN Council to perform its functions, duties and responsibilities in order to meet the goals of the SALINTUBIG project and maximize the benefits of the funds provided by the National Government.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation and the management guaranteed the audit team to develop enforcement technique such as ordinances for effective and stricter implementation of the provisions of RA No. 9003.
Further, though there is a big gap between knowledge and practice, we recommend that the Municipality intensify dissemination and enhancing awareness of constituents on RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the municipality thru the Municipal Social Welfare and Development (MSWD) Office adhere to the provisions of DSWD Department Order No. 22 in order to ensure the proper implementation of the program and ascertain the validity and propriety of the transaction.

We recommended and management agreed to prepare the criteria of selecting the farmers’ association. Further, to submit immediately to the Sanggunian Bayan a resolution for their approval to authorize the LCE to donate the tractors.

We commended management in their effort to recover the said amount and restored to the coffers of the municipality.  Continuous monitoring of collections and deposits is recommended to avoid similar cases in the future and ensure that all outstanding balances of the accountable officers are fully remitted.
Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher taking up the collections as well as government equity accounts in the books for fair presentation of financial reports.

We  recommended  that the Municipal  Accountant   religiously   comply with the timely preparation and submission of monthly BRS for the Audit Team’s timely verification of the correctness of the Cash in Bank accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure proper grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances by instructing the Municipal Accountant to:
[a] strictly monitor the cash advances of officials/employees before granting another one;
[b] make sure that said officials/employees settle their accounts within the time set by pertinent rules and regulations; and
[c] exert all efforts to enforce settlement of overdue accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasury and Municipal Accounting Offices be required to:
[a] trace the nature of the accounts Due from NGAs and Due from LGUs and send demand letters, if necessary or effect adjustment to its appropriate account classification;
[b] for Other Receivable and Loans Receivable accounts, exert effort to locate the current addresses of the borrowers and send demand letters; and
[c] if the probability of collection of these accounts is remote, file request for authority to write-off dormant receivable accounts to COA Office, duly supported with documents mentioned under paragraph 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that management:
[a] require the concerned barangays who were recipients of the fund transfer/financial assistance totaling ₽2,103,000.00 to submit fund utilization report duly certified by the concerned barangay officials together with the inspection report and certificate of project completion; and
[b] adjust the Donations account or the Government Equity account and take up the Due from LGUs in the account corresponding to the fund assistance and furnish the Audit Team a copy of the journal entry voucher.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the Other Payable and Trust Liability accounts and to draw the necessary adjustments, if any, and to maintain subsidiary ledger detailing all the information under these accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required to:
[a] Prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis; and
[b] Perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer:
[a] strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay in the verification and post-audit activities; and
[b] observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.

We recommended that management:
[a] Strictly adhere with DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017 in the preparation of Annual Investment Plan to be funded out of the 20% Development Fund to ensure that all items to be funded are development programs/projects and activities; and
[b] Instruct all concerned officials to be prudent in the utilization of the 20% development fund.

We recommended to management that:
[a] the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer, transfer the remaining unexpended balance amounting to ₽1,198,965.80 from the General Fund to Trust Fund in compliance with the aforementioned provisions of R.A. No. 10121 and COA Circular 2012-002; and
[b] the Municipal Accountant prepare a certified monthly Report on the Utilization of DRRM Fund and submit to COA on or before the 15th day after the end of each month for the Audit Team’s on-time evaluation of the fund.

We recommended that in the succeeding fiscal years, the municipality shall provide sufficient appropriation in the Annual Budget for Senior Citizens and Persons with Disabilities and for Implementation of the programs of the Local Councils for the Protection of Children as required under RA 9334, DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-59 and R.A. No. 9442 amending R.A. No. 7277.

We recommended that Management:
[a] direct the Municipal Engineer to strictly comply with the required posting of information and publicity on programs/projects/activities of government agencies and inform the Audit Team within 10 days after the award of the projects or before the start of the programs/projects/activities that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted at the project sites; and
[b] furnish the Audit Team with a list of all on-going government projects/programs/activities and those that are to be implemented during the year with complete details as stated in the Circular; and
[c] refrain from incurring expenses in violation of the afore-cited Circular as this shall be disallowed in audit and subject to administrative disciplinary action provided under penalty clause of the same COA Circular.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee and the General Services Officer to submit copies of approved contracts, purchase order, together with supporting documents within five (5) working days after their perfection regardless of the amount and Notice of Deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance to the Audit Team to ensure timely review/evaluation of the contracts and inspection of deliveries, respectively.

We recommended that management immediately insure its properties with the Government Insurance Fund of GSIS in accordance with the said Administrative Order and COA Circular to avoid any conflict and legal responsibility as regards to the proper insurance of its properties and allowing the Municipality to be indemnified from possible loss of property that it may sustain.

We recommended that management:
[a] provide for motor pool wherein unserviceable properties as well as assets still use for operations could be properly secured and safeguarded against the elements of wear and tear;
[b] create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee who will be responsible for the evaluation of the reasonable resale value of the unserviceable properties listed in the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) and the immediate disposal of said items; and
[c] promptly cause the sale of the unserviceable motor vehicles thru public auction in accordance COA Circular No. 86-264.

We recommended that management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 75-6 and all vehicles and equipment be marked with “For Official Use Only” followed by the name of the Municipality of Luna to safeguard government property against potential mismanagement and misuse.

We commended management for their continuous effort in undertaking these projects and activities for the improvement and development of municipality.  We recommend that the remaining three (3) units solar powered lights be installed to the programmed location so that the desired benefits be enjoyed by its constituents.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the persons liable to settle the audit disallowances.

b. We further recommended that Management cause the reversion of the over- replenishment of the SEF account and the interest income of the Trust Fund Account amounting to ₱1,860,441.96 and ₱137,309.24, respectively, to the General Fund.

The Municipal Accountant stated that as of December 31, 2017, the unliquidated cash advances decreased to ₱2,663,040.82.
Concerned officials and employees have been notified of their accountabilities and Management asked for the immediate settlement thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Government through the BAC stop procuring goods outrightly through direct contracting and emergency purchase, rather, consider widening its pool of suppliers to obtain the best deal for the government as required under the Procurement Act.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant, then, stated that due to the distance of other gasoline stations from the Municipality, they opted to directly purchase from AJA Petron Bulilit which is the nearest gasoline station.
For CY 2017, some supplies and materials were already purchased thru public bidding. However, some were still procured out-rightly thru direct contracting and emergency purchase.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management see to it that:
a. in succeeding transactions, construction materials be inspected first before delivery to the construction site. If not possible, then, inspection of goods be conducted on site before utilization;
b. the construction materials for “by administration” projects be accepted by the implementing unit, in this case, the Municipal Engineering Office, represented by the Project Engineer; and
c. conversion of materials be stopped.
observations.managementAction: Inspections were conducted and accepted by the implementing unit. Conversion of materials was stopped.
Management commits to look into the matter and collect/impose the said amount from the contractor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, through the MDRRMO:
a. review and evaluate the programs, projects and activities included the Investment Plan to properly formulate, implement and utilize the LDRRMF; and
b. utilize the LDRRM Fund only for disaster related activities to address the needs of the constituents in times of calamity.
observations.managementAction: The MDRRMO reviewed and evaluated the programs and projects and ensured utilization only for disaster related activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MDRRMO and MSWDO will coordinate for the proper documentation of the distribution of the food supplies.
observations.managementAction: CY 2017 DVs pertaining to various food supplies were duly supported with distribution sheet or acknowledgement receipt by the recipients.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management include in its accomplishment reports only activities within the context of Joint Circular 2012-01.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2017, only those activities related to GAD were included in the GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze the composition of the balance of the Accounts Payable and those without binding claims have to be reverted to the net asset/equity of the LGU.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant stated that they are only waiting for the new accounting system offered by the Commission before effecting the adjustments to properly reflect the accurate amount of the AP.
Unexpended balances as of year-end were no longer recognized as payables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant, make the necessary adjusting entry as follows:
Account Name Debit Credit
Road Networks 3,653,900.07
Flood Control System 2,410,555.85
Other Structures 994,549.00
CIP-Infrastructure Assets 1,436,802.20
CIP-Buildings and Other Structures 426,083.00
Government Equity 8,921,890.12
Total 8,921,890.12 8,921,890.12
observations.managementAction: Proposed adjusting journal entries were already taken up in the books. For CY 2017, completed and on-going projects were still recorded as MOOE but an adjusting entry was made to properly recognize these assets.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
1. Management, through the Municipal Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, accelerate the implementation of projects programmed yearly for the benefit of the citizenry; and
2. evaluate projects funded under MDF 2010-2014 if still implementable, otherwise, revert funds for re-appropriation.
observations.managementAction: Projects funded in previous years’ have been implemented but CY 2017 locally funded projects and LGSF-ADM projects are yet to be implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer restitute immediately the full amount of his cash shortage.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer committed to restitute the cash shortage until June 30, 2018. Restitutions were made in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere strictly to pertinent provisions of RA No. 6758 and Administrative Order No. 37 on the granting of additional assistance/compensation or other allowances not otherwise specified by law.
observations.managementAction: In CY 2017, ERCA was no longer granted to officers and employees, thus, the recommendation was no longer implemented. However, another personnel benefit named Economic Welfare Assistance was granted. A Notice of Suspension was already issued.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the accountant:
(a) prepare a journal entry voucher to record the actual cash transfer of the LDRRMF amounting to ₱1,336,476.58 to the trust liability account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
(b) transfer all succeeding balances of the account to the specific trust fund maintained for disaster risk reduction and management activities for the next five years and in any unutilized balance after five years shall revert back to the un-appropriated surplus of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction:
According to the Municipal Accountant, the prior years’ unexpended balance of LDRRMF amounting ₱1,336,476.58 was already recorded as Accounts Payable. He committed to reverse the journal entries made and record the unexepended balance to the trust liability account.
Unexpended LDRRMF balance for CY 2017 was already properly maintained in the trust liability account.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) adopt necessary measures to ensure compliance by the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to existing laws and regulations for the timely submission of accounts or reports of the LGU’s transactions, otherwise, sanctions maybe instituted, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: According to the Municipal Accountant, disbursement vouchers from January to March 2018 can now be submitted. But, due to the delayed transmittal of Report of Collections and Deposits (RCD) and Official Receipts (ORs) of the Treasury Office to the Accounting Office, RCD and ORs were only submitted during the conduct of cash examination. The Municipal Treasurer committed to issue memorandum to the collectors and staff in charge of the submission of the RCD and ORs for the prompt transmittal of the same to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management furnish the COA auditor with copies of contracts contracts, purchase orders and their supporting documents within five working days from execution in order to give time for the review, evaluation and recommendations to management, if needed.
observations.managementAction: Management committed to submit contracts to the Audit Team within five days upon perfection of the contract. Two (2) contracts were submitted within the reglementary period. However, auditorial review of these contracts revealed that some documents were not yet submitted. These were communicated to the Management but completed documents were not yet submitted to date.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant maintain additional sets of complete subsidiary ledgers for the Municipality’s special accounts and submit to the Audit Team the post- closing Trial Balance and financial statements of each special account as supporting schedules for the trial balance of General fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has no existing economic enterprise nor has the agency entered into any loan agreement with financial institution.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Chief Executive through the Municipal Fire Marshall and GSO need to follow-up the delivery of the fire truck. Know the reasons of the delay from the Federation and employ measures so that a refund of the amount paid be done if they could no longer make delivery of the fire truck.
observations.managementAction: To date, no delivery was made. The appeal period for the Notice of Disallowance has already prescribed; hence a Notice of Finality of Decision shall be issued.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements together with Bank Statements, paid checks and original copies of debit/credit memoranda.
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and collectors to prepare RCD and RAAF properly filled-up, correctly certified and well acknowledged.
We have recommended, and management agreed that the Fidelity Bond of the above Accountable Officers be immediately renewed.

We have recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to submit the required interim financial statements together with the Status of Appropriations, Allotments, and Obligations.
observations.managementAction: Financial statements were made but not timely submitted given limited personnel and voluminous transactions. Device a tool to facilitate timely recording. Time management.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report. See finding no. 8.2.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Accounting Unit maintain complete property ledger cards instead of subsidiary ledgers with details or breakdown.
observations.managementAction:
Prospectively, required PAR as doc’s attachment and advice Property officer to assign personnel to prepare ledger cards for every PAR.

Obtain list of all PPE assets and any available information in coordination with Accounting, Accountable Persons and Supply Officer-Designate.

Determine those unserviceable properties and agree if those are ready for disposal. Then prepare necessary activities and docs for disposal process. 
Prospectively, conduct semi-annual inventory verification of PAR and Supply Officer-Designates ledger cards to ensure reconciliation and tracking of each assets book value.

Simultaneously done with #2 for assets with traceable information.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report. See finding no. 2.1.

observations.managementAction: To look on available docs and reconcile on historical properties.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report. See finding no. 2.3.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the OIC - Municipal Accountant prepare lapsing schedules for all depreciable assets to support the provision for depreciation.
observations.managementAction:
Obtain list of all PPE assets and any available information in coordination with Accounting, Accountable Persons and Supply Officer-Designate.

Determine those unserviceable properties and agree if those are ready for disposal. Then prepare necessary activities and docs for disposal process.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report. See finding no. 2.4.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the LCE require the Supply Officer Designate to submit RPCPPE. Further, for the GSO - Designate and OIC - Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records.
observations.managementAction: The GSO keeps some of the files of ARE and will start to prepare and maintain the property ledger cards per category of PPE.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report. See finding no. 2.2.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management maintain separate accounts for the each of the BUB projects inorder to easily determine the balances of each BUB projects.

We have recommended that the WATSAN Council perform its functions, duties and responsibilities in order to meet the goals of the SALINTUBIG project and maximize the benefits of the funds provided by the National Government.
observations.managementAction: Awaiting completion on the Phase 2 in order to finalize guidelines for observation and facilitation of the management to which will be awarded to.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report. See finding no. 10.1.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended and the BAC agreed to:

inform the Municipal Health Officer to prepare PPMP of medicines and drugs and medical, dental and laboratory supplies based on every quarter requirement.

plan and prepare the APP with advance procurement actions in order to ensure that the goods are available prior or on the first day of the quarter; and

prepare the APP in accordance with the format approved by the GPPB which shall contain schedule of each procurement activities.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to comply with the requirements as provided in the above cited provisions of laws, rules and regulations particularly on the procurement of medicines.
observations.managementAction: PPMP’s are already prepared and incorporated in the APP which notes a quarterly purchase plan.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report. See finding no. 6.2.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that management develop enforcement technique such as ordinances for effective and stricter implementation of the provisions of RA No. 9003.

Further, though there is a big gap between knowledge and practice, we recommended that the Municipality intensify dissemination and enhancing awareness of constituents on RA No. 9003.

We recommended that management prioritize to safeguard the health of their garbage collectors, provide them with the necessary vaccines and protective gears.
observations.managementAction:
Reiterated to all barangays on proper implementation.

Protective gear is provided to garbage collectors though not regularly used. Re-emphasize the need and possible implication if not worn.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report. See finding no. 11.1.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the municipality must reflect in the disbursement vouchers, supporting and bidding documents the actual cost of the rice seeds for transparency.  Thereafter, collection from farmers must be properly receipted and recorded for accountability.
observations.managementAction: Documents already provided.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that in order to have an assurance that the Building Code of the Philippines is properly enforced within the Municipality and inorder to increase collection from building permit fees, the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Barangay Officials to enforce laws and ordinances applicable within the barangay and to coordinate with the Building Official when they notice that there are structures being built within their barangay.

We have recommended and management committed to review the Municipality’s Revenue Code and correct in order to align with the provisions to relevant laws, rules and regulations.  

Henceforth, the Local Finance Committee and Sanggunian Bayan Members, must see to it that proposed and approved tax ordinance or revenue measure are consistent and aligned with the relevant laws, rules and regulations.

We have recommended that the Municipal Assessor to conduct frequent physical surveys to verify and determine whether all real property within the province are properly listed in the assessment rolls and to exercise the functions of appraisal and assessment primarily for taxation purposes of all real properties in the municipality.
observations.managementAction:
Eng’g, MPDC and Assessor’s office staff randomly checks barangays on newly built structures. 

On-going review and preparation of revised Code.

Assessment rolls are prepared and available anytime for Treasury office reference. And i-tax is utilized as alternative and preventive measure.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management ensure and monitor the full implementation of the PPAs appropriated under the 20% Development Fund in order to address the needs of the constituents such as access to improved health, social, agriculture and economic services.  

Thereafter, we have recommended that management provide specific target dates of implementation of the PPAs identified in the AIP and target schedule for each procurement activities in the APP.
observations.reason: Reiterated inthis report. See finding No. 4.1.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to implement the Municipality’s funded Pre-Disaster Activities and projects to reduce the impact of calamity in accordance to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and to require the MDRRMC to monitor and evaluate its implementation.

We have recommended, and management agreed that they will ascertain the correctness of the Reports on Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Utilization which is vital in planning and decision making.
observations.managementAction:
To determine the actual status of fund as reference in the preparation of Pre-Disaster plan with strict implementation. To reprogram / re-align some programs.

To ensure reconciliation of information from LDRRMO with Accounting/Budget office before release of reports.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this report. See finding no. 5.2.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management for its effort in accomplishing development projects for the benefit of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: Management continuously implemented the projects as planned.
The management commits itself to strictly comply and adhere with the policies and guidelines set forth for the appropriation and utilization of 20% Economic Development Fund (EDF).

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

the concerned associations determine the available skill among the members of the association in such a way that no training is required for the implementation of the project.

the project coordinator be more committed and actively involved in assisting the association to determine the project viable in their area.
 
feasibility studies be conducted for each livelihood project and consider all stages involved from manufacturing to marketing.

financing the existing livelihood program of the association be considered, if warranted.
observations.managementAction: The designated PESO Manager coordinated with the different service provider (i.e. DOST, DTI) for the necessary trainings of the different Associations for the implementation of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommend submission of List of Tobacco Farmers as certified by the National Tobacco Farmers to validate the utilization of the fund to avoid audit suspension or disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The list of recipients presented therein includes not only tobacco farmers but also tobacco laborers and displaced tobacco farmers to tobacco laborers, including the heirs of these individuals, hence no such record is available at the National Tobacco Administration (NTA).
observations.reason: There are no available records as certified by the National Tobacco Administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Award Committee actively participates in the procurement activities of the agency and the Head of the Procuring Entity should require the BAC to issue Resolutions for all procurement activities of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) actively participates in the procurement activities of the agency and that management must strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC adopt the Negotiated Mode of Procurement for two failed biddings as this is required by law. Shopping should only be done in accordance with the conditions set forth in the procurement law.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) complied with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A 9184 on the proper conduct of Negotiated Procurement as an alternative mode of procurement in case of two (2) failed biddings.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel in-charge with the release of contracts/purchase orders to furnish the Auditor with copies of these documents, including the related supporting papers/attachments, within five (5) days after approval.
observations.managementAction: The management complied with the recommendation that copies of all contracts, variation orders, extra work orders and supplemental contracts, together with their supporting documents shall be submitted to the Auditor. These documents, if any, are attached to its respective vouchers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Bids and Award Committee conduct mandatory review to determine reasons for failed biddings and revised the terms, conditions, and specifications, including cost estimates, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) conducted a mandatory review on the terms, conditions, specifications and cost estimates for every failed bidding in compliance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of R.A. 9184.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management:

Stop the inappropriate charges made against the LDRRMF to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances. See to it that charges made against the LDRMMF only relates to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery programs and/or projects.

Strictly adhere to the standard procedural controls in the implementation of various programs relative to the implementation of pre and post disaster related activities and ensure that LDRMMF are utilized accordingly and effectively. 

Provide allocation in the general fund for the regular PS and MOOE of the DRRM Office; and

Provide supplemental budget to restore the amount of ₽1,165,192.61 to the LDRRM Special Trust Fund. Upon restoration of the fund, design plans, programs and activities that is responsive to disaster risk reduction and management activities, on which this fund is specifically intended.
observations.managementAction: The management strictly adheres to the guidelines on the utilization of 5% MDRRMF.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management strictly implement the above-mentioned provisions of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular No. 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Also, require the officials and employees concerned to immediately liquidate in full their outstanding cash advances and to make appropriate action for those who failed to settle the accounts.
observations.managementAction: The management will not allow any further Cash Advances for those Accountable Officers who were not able to settle yet his account. The management will strictly comply with the guidelines on the Grants, Utilization and Liquidation of Cash Advances.
observations.reason: Mr. Carlos G. Lampitoc declined to settle his accounts with the LGU because he claimed that he already submitted his liquidation report to the former Municipal Accountant, Ms. Lolita Cadiz.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to reconcile the book and bank balances in compliance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and other pertinent rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The necessary adjustment was made directly to the Government Equity account.

observations.recommendation: To maintain a strong internal control for this transaction, transparency and to avoid existence of ghost employees in the future, we recommended that payment of salaried and wages be paid through LBP ATM Payroll system.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation that payment of Salaries and Wages of regular and casual employees be paid through LBP ATM Payroll system is not practical for its employees considering the distance of LBP Cauayan City branch and any nearby ATM of LBP, and since LBP was not amenable with the previous request of the management to install ATM at the Municipal Compound. As stated on the AOM on the other hand, the grant and liquidation of cash advance for the payment of Salaries and Wages of Regular and Casual employees was properly handled by the concerned officials, hence the current practice is hereby requested and the management commits itself to make sure that strong internal control be maintained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer stop the scheme and not to connive with the regular employees applying for loans in making it appear that NTHP is greater than the actual amount. The action is purposely to deceive the lending institution and conduct not proper for government officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: The management is no longer allowing additional loans for employees whose Net Take Home Pay is below the minimum amount of ₽4,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant analyze the composition of the balance of the Accounts Payable and those without binding claims have to be reverted to the net asset/equity of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Huge amount refers to the unimplemented projects under the 20% Development Fund.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

demand the concerned beneficiaries to return the equipment and materials intended for their livelihood projects; and



monitor regularly the implementation of the projects to ensure compliance with the terms and conditions of the Agreement.
observations.managementAction: No action was undertaken by management on this issue.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adhere strictly to pertinent provisions of RA No. 6758 and Administrative Order No. 37 on the granting of additional assistance/compensation or other allowances not otherwise specified by law.
observations.managementAction: Management granted another Economic Relief Assistance due to Super Typhoon Lawin to each officers and employees of the Municipality in CY 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management, through the BAC, consider widening its pool of suppliers to obtain the best deal for the government and adhere strictly with pertinent provisions of RA No. 9184 in the conduct of procurement transactions.
observations.managementAction: The BAC was not able to canvass since these are emergency in nature.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the concerned officials to settle promptly their suspensions and disallowances.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant will review/check the suspension/ disallowances issued.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

exert best effort to trace and review his records and substantiate the unaccounted reconciling items of the Cash in Bank accounts pertaining to prior years and submit Bank Reconciliation Statements as prescribed by regulations; and

prepare the necessary adjusting entries for reconciling items identified in the books in order to establish the correct balances of the Cash in Bank accounts.
observations.managementAction: Committed to adjust the affected accounts this second quarter of 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended  that the accountant:

prepare a journal entry voucher to record the actual cash transfer of the LDRRMF amounting to ₱1,336,476.58 to the trust liability account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002; and
   
transfer all succeeding balances of the account to the specific trust fund maintained for disaster risk reduction and management activities for the next five years and any unutilized balance after five years shall revert back to the un-appropriated surplus of the General Fund.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant admitted that he did not transfer the balance to a special trust fund account due to the reprogramming made by the Designated MDRRMO.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant maintain additional sets of complete subsidiary ledgers for the Municipality’s special accounts and submit to the Audit Team the post- closing Trial Balance and financial statements of each special account as supporting schedules for the trial balance of General fund.
observations.managementAction: No action made.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the LCE agreed to enjoin the Inventory Committee to include in the conduct of inventory of the properties of the municipality to the infrastructures and other community facilities.  We likewise recommended to the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit the required Report to the DILG and the Municipal Accountant to complete the Registry of Public Infrastructure and disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction:
Coordinate to the Mun. Engineering Office and BAC Office for retrieving  previous files especially infrastructure projects.
Assessor’s Office shall be included also.
observations.reason: Hired one job order as Engineering Aide to support the voluminous work in the office.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipality consider:

a.	Integrating   the recommendations of the DENR  – Environmental Management Bureau in their 10-year SWM Plan;
b.	Developing enforcement technique for effective and stricter implementation of ordinances on solid waste management; and
c.	Enhancing awareness of the stakeholders on existing laws, rules and regulations on solid waste management and strictly observe the provisions thereof.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in this AAR, see finding number 6.
observations.reason: To conduct re- assessment of waste segregation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the LCE to direct the Treasury Office to keep files of photocopied required documents  and to properly fill-up the form for application of business permits inorder to validate the true and correct amounts assessed and collected.
observations.managementAction: To keep files of photocopied required documents and to require  all business owners to fill-up all information in the business application form.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and the GSO Designate agreed to submit copies of the Contracts and POs and supporting documents and delivery receipts and Acceptance and Inspection Reports to the Auditor within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction: To submit PO’s and contracts and other supporting documents on the prescribed period
observations.reason: POs/Contracts of goods acquired were not submitted to COA prior to the actual delivery/purchase.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended and management committed to submit their revenue and disbursement documents on time to facilitate the review and audit of the transactions.
observations.managementAction: To submit revenue and disbursement documents on prescribed period.
observations.reason:
Revenue and disbursement documents were not submitted on time to facilitate the review and
audit of the transactions.

observations.recommendation: The team recommended and management promised to revisit the completed projects during the  one (1) year defects liability period and issue the Final Certificate of Acceptance.  To compel the contractors to repair if there are defects due to use of inferior materials or to forfeit the Performance Security if there are defects noted.  Lastly, to require the Contractor to issue or submit Warranty Security to ensure that he will be liable for Structural Defects if there are any.
observations.reason: Revisited the completed projects and issued Final Certificate of Acceptance. Compelled the contractors to repair any defects and required them to submit Warranty Security.

observations.recommendation:
We have reiterated our prior year’s recommendations and management promised that:

a. After the budget proposal has been approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity and/or other oversight bodies, the procuring entity’s budget office will furnish a copy of the procuring entity’s budget proposal as well as the corresponding PPMPs to the  BAC Secretariat for its review  and consolidation into the proposed APP;
b. The APP will include not only common use supplies but all the municipality’s programs/projects/activities; and
c. Further, the correct format be  used as prescribed by the GPPB.
observations.reason: Only PPMP for office supplies was prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to maintain separate accounts for the each of the BUB projects and said appropriation must be  continued and remain valid until fully spent or the BUB projects are 100% implemented or completed.

Lack of knowledge in the preparation of the BRS will be substantiated through reading and asking information from the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to effect the required reconciliation and to perform the same regularly, as mandated in Section 181 (c) of the GAAM, Volume I to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation on the foregoing-stated PPEs to correctly present their balances in the financial statements.  Maintenance of records on the PPEs like ledgers cards and other schedules must be done; and conduct appraisal, through an appraisal committee, the value and useful life of the subject PPEs as basis for the computation of depreciation expense in case historical data of said PPEs are no longer available.
observations.managementAction: No management action
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: Stop the payment of benefits to its officials, employees and job orders without legal basis; submit to the Auditor legal basis on the receipt of the benefits and justification, if there is any, on the occurrence of the irregularity; advice the Accountant to record in the books the financial transactions in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: Ensure that money under Trust Fund shall always be used for specific purpose.  Submit to the Auditor justification/explanation on the occurrence of the irregularity.  Strictly adhere to Section 94 of NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1 and Section 4(3) of P.D. 1445 in the proper recording and utilization of amounts under Trust Fund.
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: Formulate a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP.  Appropriate at least 5% of the total estimated revenue for LDRRMF to have ample fund to defray expenses for the reduction of disaster risks and enhancement of disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the municipality; transfer from general fund ample, if not all, cash allocation to the LDRRMF to fund all its identified programs and activities.  Accrue and transfer the unexpended portion of the LDRRMF at the end of the year to a special trust fund in compliance with Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121; plan, develop and implement only PPAs that are implementable in a given period.
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: Finish as soon as possible its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan and submit the same to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board for review and integration in the provincial solid waste management plan; create a Municipal Waste Management Board in a manner and for the purpose stated in Section 12 of R.A. No. 9003; implement the rules and procedures on proper segregation, collection, transportation and disposition of solid waste materials as prescribed in Sections 22, 23, 24, and 37 of the Act.
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: Submit to the DSWD, in a manner prescribed in the MOA, the updated list of eligible senior citizens, monthly accomplishment report of the program, SORD, List of paid and unpaid beneficiaries certified by the municipal accountant and signed by the municipal mayor and the checks for refund of the unexpended balance; and adhere strictly to the provisions in the MOA on the receipt and utilization of funds intended for the social pension program.

observations.recommendation: Ensure that cash advances shall be drawn for specific purpose.
