observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, effect the following adjusting entries to correct the Cash In Bank balance of the six accounts:






We further recommended that Management cause the reversion of the over-replenishment of the SEF account and the interest income of the Trust Fund Account amounting to ₱1,860,441.96 and ₱137,309.24, respectively, to the General Fund.

We recommended that Management:
monitor cash advances to avoid accumulation; and
notify the concerned officials and employees of their accountabilities and demand for the immediate settlement thereof, including imposing sanctions or withholding of salaries in case of non-compliance, pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular 97-002 and Section 89 of PD 14.

We recommended that Management through the BAC stop procuring goods out-rightly through direct contracting and emergency purchase, rather, consider widening its pool of suppliers to obtain the best deal for the government as required under the Procurement Act.

We recommended that Management see to it that:

in succeeding transactions, construction materials be inspected first before delivery to the construction site. If not possible, then, inspection of goods be conducted on site before utilization;
the construction materials for “by administration” projects be accepted by the implementing unit, in this case, the Municipal Engineering Office, represented by the Project Engineer; and
conversion of materials be stopped.

We recommended that Management, through the MDRRMO:
review and evaluate the programs, projects and activities included the Investment Plan to properly formulate, implement and utilize the LDRRMF; and
utilize the LDRRM Fund only for disaster related activities to address the needs of the constituents in times of calamity.
We recommended that the MDRRMO and MSWDO will coordinate for the proper documentation of the distribution of the food supplies.

We recommended that the Municipal Social Worker and the concerned Day Care Worker prepare daily menu, and monitoring and growth chart of the beneficiaries as a tool for evaluating the effectiveness of the program. Further, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor assign a person-in-charge to monitor the Supplemental Feeding.
We recommended that Management include in its accomplishment reports only activities within the context of Joint Circular 2012-01.
We recommended that management require the concerned officials and employees to settle promptly their suspensions and disallowances.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly  Bank Reconciliation Statements together with Bank Statements, paid checks and original copies of debit/credit memoranda.
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer and collectors to prepare RCD and RAAF properly filled-up, correctly certified and well acknowledged.
We have recommended and management agreed that the Fidelity Bond of the above Accountable Officers be immediately renewed.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer to submit the required interim financial statements together with the Status of Appropriations, Allotments, and Obligations.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Accounting Unit maintain complete property ledger cards instead of subsidiary ledgers with details or breakdown.
We have recommended that the LCE direct the:
Accountable Officers to return to the GSO Designate the unserviceable properties;
GSO Designate to:
cancel the ARE related to the unserviceable properties turned-over to her and issue ARE to all property accountable officials and employees and keep copies thereof;
initiate the disposal in accordance with the requirements provided in COA Circular No. 92-386 dated October 20, 1992 and Section 79 of PD No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration due to natural elements and loss of value, generate additional funds, if found valuable and sold.
We have recommended that the OIC - Municipal Accountant prepare lapsing schedules for all depreciable assets to support the provision for depreciation.
We have recommended that the LCE require the Supply Officer Designate to submit RPCPPE. Further, for the GSO - Designate and OIC - Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records.

We have recommended that management maintain separate accounts for the each of the BUB projects inorder to easily determine the balances of each BUB projects.
We have recommended that the WATSAN Council perform its functions, duties and responsibilities in order to meet the goals of the SALINTUBIG project and maximize the benefits of the funds provided by the National Government.

We have recommended and the BAC agreed to:
inform the Municipal Health Officer to prepare PPMP of medicines and drugs and medical, dental and laboratory supplies based on every quarter requirement.
plan and prepare the APP with advance procurement actions in order to ensure that the goods are available prior or on the first day of the quarter; and
prepare the APP in accordance with the format approved by the GPPB which shall contain schedule of each procurement activities.
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the BAC to comply with the requirements as provided in the above cited provisions of laws, rules and regulations particularly on the procurement of medicines.

We have reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that management develop enforcement technique such as ordinances for effective and stricter implementation of the provisions of RA No. 9003.
Further, though there is a big gap between knowledge and practice, we recommended that the Municipality intensify dissemination and enhancing awareness of constituents on RA No. 9003.
We recommended that management prioritize to safeguard the health of their garbage collectors, provide them with the necessary vaccines and protective gears.

We have recommended that the municipality must reflect in the disbursement vouchers, supporting and bidding documents the actual cost of the rice seeds for transparency.  Thereafter, collection from farmers must be properly receipted and recorded for accountability.

We have recommended that in order to have an assurance that the Building Code of the Philippines is properly enforced within the Municipality and inorder to increase collection from building permit fees, the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Barangay Officials to enforce laws and ordinances applicable within the barangay and to coordinate with the Building Official when they notice that there are structures being built within their barangay.
We have recommended and management committed to review the Municipality’s Revenue Code and correct in order to align with the provisions to relevant laws, rules and regulations.
We have recommended that the Municipal Assessor to  conduct frequent physical surveys to verify and determine whether all real property within the province are properly listed in the assessment rolls and to exercise the functions of appraisal and assessment primarily for taxation purposes of all real properties in the municipality.

We have recommended that management ensure and monitor the full implementation of the PPAs appropriated under the 20% Development Fund in order to address the needs of the constituents such as access to improved health, social, agriculture and economic services.
Thereafter, we have recommended that management provide specific target dates of implementation of the PPAs identified in the AIP and target schedule for each procurement activities in the APP.

We have recommended to the Local Chief Executive to implement the Municipality’s funded Pre-Disaster Activities and projects to reduce the impact of calamity in accordance to Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and to require the MDRRMC to monitor and evaluate its implementation.
We have recommended and management agreed that they  will ascertain the correctness of the Reports on Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Utilization which is vital in planning and decision making.

We recommended for the management to immediately settle the above suspension which was settled during at year-end. Henceforth, for the Municipal Accountant to require complete documentation of all financial transactions to avoid suspensions in the post-audit of accounts by the audit team.

We recommended that:
the concerned associations determine the available skill among the members of the association in such a way that no training is required for the implementation of the project.
the project coordinator be more committed and actively involved in assisting the association to determine the project viable in their area.
feasibility studies be conducted for each livelihood project and consider all stages involved from manufacturing to marketing.
financing the existing livelihood program of the association be considered, if warranted.
We recommend submission of List of Tobacco Farmers as certified by the National Tobacco Farmers to validate the utilization of the fund to avoid audit suspension or disallowance.

We recommended that the Bids and Award Committee actively participates in the procurement activities of the agency and the Head of the Procuring Entity should require the BAC to issue Resolutions for all procurement activities of the LGU.

We recommended that the BAC adopt the Negotiated Mode of Procurement for two failed biddings as this is required by law. Shopping should only be done in accordance with the conditions set forth in the procurement law.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the personnel in-charge with the release of contracts/purchase orders to furnish the Auditor with copies of these documents, including the related supporting papers/attachments, within five (5) days after approval.

We recommended that the Bids and Award Committee conduct mandatory review to determine reasons for failed biddings and revised the terms, conditions, and specifications, including cost estimates, if necessary.

We recommended that the Management:
Stop the inappropriate charges made against the LDRRMF to avoid audit suspensions and disallowances. See to it that charges made against the LDRMMF only relates to disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery programs and/or projects.
Strictly adhere to the standard procedural controls in the implementation of various programs relative to the implementation of pre and post disaster related activities and ensure that LDRMMF are utilized accordingly and effectively.
Provide allocation in the general fund for the regular PS and MOOE of the DRRM Office; and
Provide supplemental budget to restore the amount of P1,165,192.61 to the LDRRM Special Trust Fund. Upon restoration of the fund, design plans, programs and activities that is responsive to disaster risk reduction and management activities, on which this fund is specifically intended.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management strictly implement the above mentioned provisions of Section 89 of P.D. 1445 and COA Circular 97-002 in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Also, require the officials and employees concerned to immediately liquidate in full their outstanding cash advances and to make appropriate action for those who failed to settle the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a reconciliation report of reciprocal accounts and take up the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to ensure that all reconciling items are resolved and/or adjusted on a timely basis and perform constant review and appraisal of transactions to deter error and omissions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to reconcile the book and bank balances in compliance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and other pertinent rules and regulations.

However, to maintain a strong internal control for this transaction, transparency and to avoid existence of ghost employees in the future, we recommended that payment of salaried and wages be paid through LBP ATM Payroll system.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer stop the scheme and not to connive with the regular employees applying for loans in making it appear that NTHP is greater than the actual amount. The action is purposely to deceive the lending institution and conduct not proper for government officials and employees.

We recommended and Managementinstructed the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the bank reconciliation statements especially for General Fund and Trust Fund as required under Section 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011 dated October 2, 1996 and Section 74 of PD 1445.

We recommended and Management agreed to constitute an inventory committee/team that will spearhead in planning and conducting physical count of the Municipality’s supplies and properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team Report on Physical Count of PPE pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the Supplies and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets; advise the Storekeeper to maintain and update Supplies and Property Cards per category of materials and property, plant and equipment, including the unserviceable properties, and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the accounting records; install control to keep safe the supplies/materials and PPEs of the municipality like the preparation and maintenance of Requisition and Issue Slips/Vouchers, the practice of property tagging for property identification and monitoring, maintenance of ARE, etc.; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in the above-quoted regulations and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended and Management directed the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation on the foregoing-stated PPEs to correctly present their balances in the financial statements.  Maintenance of records on the PPEs like ledgers cards and other schedules must be done; and conduct appraisal, through an appraisal committee, the value and useful life of the subject PPEs as basis for the computation of depreciation expense in case historical data of said PPEs are no longer available.

We recommended and Management directed the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the financial accounts of the LGU.  These ledgers shall be the source of information to prepare schedules to facilitate verification of the accounts in the financial statements; advise the Municipal Accountant to equip herself the technical knowledge and skills on the preparation of the ledgers by reading prevailing manuals and issuances on the matter and asking information from the Auditor; and strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 4.8, 111.1, 114 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445.

We recommended and Managementinstructed the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the unutilized balances of LDRRMF for CYs 2015 and 2016 from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund in the total amount of P5,910,736.70in accordance with Item 5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and Item 6 of the NDRRMC-DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013.
We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to observe the requirements set forth under COA Circular No. 2012-002 specifically the guidelines on the accounting and reporting of LDRRMF to ascertain that the financial information on LDRRMF are taken up appropriately in the books and properly disclosed in the Financial Statements.

We recommended and Management instructed the Municipal Accountant the timely recognition and recording of interest income earned and interest expenses incurred for each quarter of the year to ensure the reliability of financial statements. Further, to correct the understatement of the cash, income and expense accounts, effect the journal entry suggested below:
Cash in Bank                   16,499.63
Interest Expense                4,124.91
Interest Income                   20,624.54

We recommended and Management agreed to submit to the Auditor the lacking documents mentioned in the attached schedule. All claims shall always be supported with complete documentation; Faithfully adhere to all laws and regulations applicable to financial transactions of any government agency. Submit to the Auditor justification/explanation on the occurrence of the above-noted irregularities.

We recommended and Management agreed tostop granting communication allowance to employees other than the department heads and elected officials of the Agency pursuant to COA Circular No 2012-003. Cause the refund of the amount disbursed to replenish the depleted fund. If refund was made, utilize the fund to a more important activity like purchase of drugs and medicines in order to attain one (1) of the objectives of the fund.

We recommended and Managementrequired the Municipal Health Officer to attach a certification that the drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF Volume I, current edition in compliance with Paragraph II.2 of DOH AO No. 163 s. 2002.

We recommended and Management agreed tostop disbursing PCF in payment of various claims of its employees and suppliers if these are not properly supported with the required pertinent documents. Likewise, submit the required pertinent documents to the Office of the Auditor to evaluate the validity of the disbursements. Otherwise, a Notice of Suspension shall be issued.

We recommended and Management agreed to convene annuallywiththe Local School Board and prepare the required annual school board budget identifying therein the priority programs/projects/activities chargeable to Special Education Fund in accordance with the provisions of Sections 98 (b) (3) and 99 (a) and 100 of Republic Act No. 7160.
Further, Management agreed to stop the practice of disbursing funds without budget. Disbursements without budgets render transactions illegal. Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to refrain from processing claims against Special Education Fund unless there is an approved annual school board budget shall. When such shall be prepared, disbursements should always be based on the priority programs/projects/activities identified from it.

We recommended and Managementrequired the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) to prepare and submit to the concerned offices the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which shall present the 30 percent allocation for Quick Response Fund in lump-sum and the 70 percent allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to priority projects and activities to be implemented in the ensuing year pursuant to Section 12( c)and 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and Managementrequired the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer’s Office to submit the supporting documents necessary to validate the payments. Further, stop paying claims of suppliers without proper supporting documents.

We recommended and Managementrequired the Municipal Engineer to ensure that all PPAs are provided with the prescribed tarpaulin signboards with the project status in order to promote good governance and secure the right of the people to information on matters of public concern.

We recommended that Managementsubmit its 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan to the Provincial Solid Waste Management Board for review and integration in the provincial solid waste management plan; implement the rules and procedures on proper segregation, collection, transportation and disposal of solid waste materials as prescribed in Sections 17, 21, 22, 23, 24, 32 and 37 of the Act; and adhere to the provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 in the creation, implementation and monitoring of the LGU solid waste management.

We recommendedand Management agreed toconduct competitive bidding in all its procurement as required in Section 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.  Alternative methods of procurement shall only be used when conditions allow; submit to this Office the justification on the occurrence of the irregularity.  Also, submit the DVs and its supporting documents pertaining to the 47 checks with a total amount of P1,130,314.59 and furnish the Audit Team a certification from VMLP that the donated amount was indeed received and the other lacking documents mentioned above.  Strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9184 and PD 1445.

We recommend that Management make certain that all barangay shares from the Real Property collections are remitted to the LGUs concerned within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA 7160.

We recommend Management strict compliance of the Omnibus Guidelines in the implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program to ensure efficient and effective implementation and attainment of its objectives. Feeding should be made continuously for 120 days to show and measure the impact of the program. Further, the observations discussed above under the implementing guidelines/scheme of the program shall be given special attention in compliance with the Omnibus guidelines in the implementation of the Supplementary Feeding Program. The allocation per child per day shall be increased to P15.00 in compliance with the Memorandum of Agreement.

The Municipal Mayor to establish and/or strengthen the institutional mechanisms as required in JMC No. 2013-01 by reconstituting the GFPS in accordance with the structure and composition in the MCW-IRR which includes a TWG; directing the Municipal Planning and Development Office to set up GAD Database and maintain the same; and once established and strengthened, the GFPS are enjoined to thoroughly review the GPB to ensure that it addresses concrete gender issues/GAD mandate and that attribution from regular PPAs are gender-responsive.
We recommended that Managementinstruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate remittance of the taxes withheld to the BIR pursuant to the above cited regulations.
We recommended that Management should look for the said notices and comply/settle the sameimmediately.

observations.managementAction: The management made the submission of reports on the scheduled date and ensures the completeness of signatures and supporting documents of each vouchers prior to its disbursement.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the    
Municipal Accountant and

strictly comply with the

Constitute an inventory committee/team that will spearhead in planning and conducting physical count of the Municipality’s supplies and properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team Report on Physical Count of PPE pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the Supplies and Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets; advise the Storekeeper to maintain and update Supplies and Property Cards per category of materials and property, plant and equipment, including the unserviceable properties, and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the accounting records.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the financial accounts of the LGU; advise the Municipal Accountant to equip herself the technical knowledge and skills on the preparation of the ledgers by reading prevailing manuals and issuances on the matter and asking information from the Auditor.
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: Make sure that all job orders shall be covered with job order contracts; seek SB authority/resolution allowing the Municipal Mayor to enter into contract with job orders and to incur obligation in paying the wages of the same; ensure that all payments of job orders’ wages shall always be supported with DTRs and accomplishment reports and to submit the same to the Accounting before the preparation of the payroll to make sure that payments thereof will pertain to actual no. of days worked; instruct the Municipal Accountant to make corresponding adjusting entry to correct the erroneous entry made and to record in the books all the financial transactions in accordance with the accounting rules and standards.
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: Support all claims with complete documents; faithfully adhere to all laws and regulations applicable to financial transactions of any government agency; ensure that all financial transactions shall be recorded in the books in accordance with the prevailing accounting rules and standards.  Instruct the Accountant to make the suggested adjusting journal entries in the books.
observations.reason: Not implemented

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Municipal Accountant to record the financial assistance granted to various schools by debiting receivable accounts like “Due from NGA” account instead of having it expensed outright to maintain accountability of the funds; approve payments only for expenses authorized under Section 272 of R.A. 7160.    Submit to the Auditor justification/explanation for non-compliance; ensure that all financial assistance granted to various schools shall be supported with official receipts; support all disbursements with information as to the purpose of the payments.  Make sure that all payments for various activities must have corresponding budget.  Make certain that all financial assistance should be supported with program/activity design or breakdown of expenses.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: Stop the practice of charging against the 20% development fund the expenditures which do not have direct contribution to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures.  refrain from charging against the fund the expenditures which do not have corresponding appropriation to comply with Section 4.1 of P.D. No. 1445; provide the Auditors with documents as proof that the cash assistance and cash transfer made in the amount of P11,800.00 and P220,000.00, respectively, were all spent in accordance with the objectives set forth in the DBM and DILG JMC No. 2011-1.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: Conduct competitive bidding in all its procurement as required in Section 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.  Alternative methods of procurement shall only be used when conditions allow; submit to this Office the justification on the occurrence of the irregularity.  Also furnish the Audit Team the lacking documents mentioned above.  Failure to comply may cause the suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and strictly adhere to the provision of Section 10 of R.A. 9184.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: Request from the legislative department to issue a resolution authorizing the Mayor to open a trust account for the exclusive use of the DSWD supplementing feeding program in compliance with the MOA; ensure compliance with R.A. 9184 in the purchase of materials needed in the feeding program; advise the municipal treasurer to read the provisions in the MOA to acquaint herself with the requirements to comply.  She must liquidate immediately every funds received after their implementation; consolidate and submit to the DSWD field offices, copy furnished the provincial offices, the monthly physical and financial report and quarterly report on nutritional status of children beneficiaries in compliance with the requirement in the MOA.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: Establish GFPS to lead in mainstreaming GAD PPAs in LGU’s various policies, plans and programs; formulate and include only programs, projects and activities that would support government’s commitment to promote equality among women and men to resources and opportunities and which specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; implement only the PPAs that were in the GPB with due consideration of the cost as therein budgeted; and prepare, maintain and submit to the concerned government oversight agencies the GPB and GAR in a manner and in prescribed forms to comply Sections 4.1.C.1 and 4.1.C.8.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: Delegate some of the MPDC’s works and obligations to other competent municipal employee so that the former may find more time to function as a municipal engineer.  Hasten the implementation of the project so that people could immediately benefit therefrom; ensure that every program, project or activity received from any sources should be immediately commenced in accordance with the guidelines and standards governing thereof so that benefit therefrom will be promptly availed; and make sure that every project, program or activity of the LGU should be kept/maintained with complete records or documents like the project proposal, program of work, detailed engineering design, bidding documents, accomplishment reports, billings, paid vouchers and the like.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: Implement the programs, projects and activities as scheduled in order that benefits will be availed by the beneficiaries on time. Plan and develop only PPAs that are implementable in a given period.
observations.reason: Partially implemented

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to effect the required reconciliation and to perform the same regularly, as mandated in Section 181 (c) of the GAAM, Volume I to present fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: To reconcile cash balances
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

As of December 31, 2015, a total of P4,069,237.10 variance in cash accounts remained unadjusted.

observations.recommendation: Create and direct the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all the Municipality’s supplies/materials and property and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the RPCI and RPCPPE pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update supplies and PPE Ledger Cards for each category of assets; advise the Storekeeper to maintain and update Stock and Property Cards per category of supplies and property and to reconcile balances thereof with that of the accounting records; and adhere to provisions on supplies and property management set forth in the above-quoted regulations and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Conduct inventory
observations.reason:
Not implemented

The Agency still did not conduct inventory for CY 2015.  Reiterated in Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: Maintain subsidiary ledgers for receivable and payable accounts and other accounts prescribed in Sections 114 and 10 of P.D. 1445 and NGAS Manual; direct the Accountant-Designate to exert more effort in determining the persons/funds accountable for the immediate liquidation/settlement of the amount; and adhere strictly to Sections 111 and 114 of P.D. 1445 and Section 10 of the NGAS Manual in the keeping of the accounts of the government.
observations.reason:
Not implemented

The Receivable and Payable accounts in CY 2015 were not supported with subsidiary ledgers and other schedules.

observations.recommendation: Reclassify the financial assistance from Due to Other Funds to Due to Other NGAs and adhere strictly to Section 10 of COA Circular No. 94-013 in recording future fund transfers transactions.
observations.managementAction: To reclassify erroneous entry

observations.recommendation: Strictly adhere with COA Circular No. 2009-001 on the submission of perfected contracts and purchase orders together with the related supporting documents to COA for review.
observations.managementAction: For submission to Auditors

Not implemented

Duly executed contracts remained unsubmitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate books of account for the 20% Development Fund and prepare separate financial statements for the said special account as prescribed in Section 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volume I; require the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan to issue a resolution for the creation of a separate depository accounts for the 20% Development Fund to facilitate the recording of receipts and disbursements of cash in the prescribed cashbook; and strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 313 of R.A. 7160 and Section 110 of NGAS Manual in the creation, recording and reporting of special accounts.
observations.managementAction: To provide separate books of account
observations.reason: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: Require the Members of the SB to issue a resolution or secure copy of Presidential declaration stating the LGU of General Luna under the state of calamity for certain periods in CY 2014 as prescribed in the NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1.
observations.reason: Fully implemented

observations.recommendation: Submit to the Audit Team all the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents of the transactions stated above; instruct the Municipal Engineer to determine the location of the DOH Salintubig project and to assist the Audit Team in the conduct of ocular inspection of the same; always maintain complete records of the project; and adhere to provisions of Sections 2 and 111 of P.D.1445 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations in the implementation of projects as well as keeping of its accounts and records.
observations.reason:
Not implemented

The required documents remained unsubmitted to COA.  Ocular inspection of the project was not done.  Information received in this Office showed that the project was transferred to another barangay beneficiary.

observations.recommendation: Maintain separate containers/ receptacles for each type of waste for temporary placement of solid waste prior to its final hauling to an open dump site, and which should be properly marked or identified as "compostable", "non-recyclable", "recyclable" or "special waste”; require the collectors and other personnel directly dealing with the collection of solid waste to wear personal protective equipment and paraphernalia, such as mask, rubber boots and the like; transport solid waste using trucks/haulers with covers; and convert the open dump to a controlled dump site.
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

The municipality had already formed a Municipal Waste Management Board.  Preparation of the SWM Plan will commence very soon.

observations.recommendation: Submit the required financial statements and reports to the Audit Team in a manner and frequency required in Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and Chapter 4 of NGAS Manual for LGU.
observations.managementAction: To submit to COA the needed accounts
observations.reason:
Partially implemented

Some of the monthly, quarterly, semi-annual and annual reports were regularly submitted to COA.

The municipality is gradually complying in the submission of the monthly, quarterly and annual reports.  Disbursement vouchers are supported with integral documents.  Most of the department heads are competent enough to answer queries.

observations.recommendation: Submit the GAD Plan and Budget to the DILG for review and approval and to formulate and include programs, projects and activities that would only address women’s human rights and gender discrimination in the government policies, programs and procedures, in a manner prescribed in PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.reason:
Not implemented

observations.recommendation: Keep intact all reports/records of each of the programs, projects and activities it is implementing in order that monitoring thereof by government officials and employees concerned ensures determination whether the PPAs had been efficiently, economically and effectively managed.

.
